DEVLOG.md:104:**2026-06-11 03:30:00Z** - **AUDIT**: Design-coherence Phase B executed and delivered (`docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md`; 164 agents, ~7.0M tokens). 8 dimension reviewers (boundaries/duplication/errors/constants/async/deadcode/tests/drift) re-derived mechanisms from code, then every finding faced 2 adversarial verifiers (mechanism + impact lens) with tiebreakers. 76 reported → 70 confirmed / 6 refuted; ~28 severity downgrades (impact lens strictest). Effective HIGHs (4): network buffer-pool budget is a frozen streams*2+4 formula authorizing multi-GiB with no memory awareness (3 pasted sites; OOM-by-constant); live pull-sync computes full tuning then discards all but chunk_bytes (1 stream + pool=4 vs push's 32 streams); deprecated Pull RPC half-migration (~500 unreachable daemon lines that are ALSO the only multi-stream pull); blanket #[cfg(unix)] on remote transfer tests with nothing unix-specific (Windows parity untestable). Notable mediums: log:: facade has NO backend anywhere (all log::warn/error discarded); workspace-root tests/ never compiled by cargo; daemon compute phases blind to client disconnect; 2 more detach-on-drop families beyond design-2. Refuted kills recorded in the doc so they are not re-found (2 of 6 were 'textually true but unreachable' — same lesson as the Phase A erratum). At the Phase B checkpoint: owner go/no-go for Phase C synthesis.
DEVLOG.md:106:**2026-06-11 02:40:00Z** - **AUDIT**: Phase A bug-class candidates dispositioned (owner delegated the call) + Phase B launched (owner: "Full Phase B"). Each of the map's four immediate candidates was re-verified by hand before filing. Three confirmed → filed as `[ ]` Open: `design-1-cli-pull-byte-double-count` (Medium; pipeline.rs:233-235 reports full bytes on BOTH Payload and FileComplete, blit-cli/transfers/remote.rs fold adds both → ~2x), `design-2-orphaned-daemon-data-planes` (High; bare JoinHandles at daemon pull.rs:180/:297 + push/control.rs:57 detach on control-stream death; AbortOnDrop fix exists but is pub(crate) in blit-core and was never propagated), `design-3-unbounded-data-plane-connects` (Medium; TcpStream::connect with no timeout at data_plane.rs:92 + pull.rs:1710 — audit-2's fix never reached the data plane). The fourth (>4 MiB manifest batch) was REFUTED in mechanism: ENUM_BATCH_SIZE=500 batches an internal channel, legacy Pull sends one header per PullChunk (service/pull.rs:583), push need-list is byte-capped at 512 KiB (push/control.rs:25-26); erratum recorded in the map under headline 8, residual decode-limit/tar-shard-manifest question handed to Phase B. Lesson institutionalized: Phase B verifier prompts instruct refutation-by-reading-code, never trust-the-map. Git: owner approved push; `master` pushed to origin + gitea at `ab0d8a0` (was 1be16bc).
DEVLOG.md:110:**2026-06-11 00:42:30Z** - **REVIEW**: Verification and acceptance of `audit-h3c-slice1-grpc-fallback-frame-contract` (commit `bf4cc82`, verdict declared by owner). Validation suite re-run at HEAD `1be16bc`: fmt/clippy/test all green (exit 0), blit-core test-function count identical at `bf4cc82` and HEAD (344) — no test drop. The review assessment surfaced four facts the finding doc didn't know, recorded here as input to the slice-2 re-scope: **(1)** the 1 MiB frame cap is load-bearing correctness, not just cadence — tonic 0.14's default 4 MiB decode limit (`DEFAULT_MAX_RECV_MESSAGE_SIZE`, no `max_decoding_message_size` override anywhere in the workspace) means pre-slice-1 gRPC-fallback transfers of files >4 MiB should fail at decode, since `tuning.rs` emits 16/32/64 MiB chunks and `pull_sync.rs:515` passed `tuning.chunk_bytes` straight into `GrpcServerStreamingSink`. Version-skew residual: a new client against an old daemon still receives uncapped frames. **(2)** The bigger H3 hole is missing client-side HTTP/2 keepalive: daemon pings clients (`main.rs:138`) but all three client channel builders (`pull.rs:239`, `push/client/mod.rs:307`, `blit-app/src/client.rs:38` — already-drifted near-duplicates) set only `connect_timeout`, so a dead daemon hangs `message().await` forever; ~3 lines of `Endpoint` keepalive config cover dead-peer/black-hole at transport level and shrink slice 2's watchdog scope to wedged-but-alive peers only. **(3)** Fallback throughput is governed by HTTP/2 flow-control windows, not frame size — hyper defaults: client 2 MiB stream / 5 MiB conn; daemon server 1 MiB conn total → hardware-independent push-fallback ceiling on high-BDP links; `http2_adaptive_window(true)` is the no-tuning fix. **(4)** `clamp_fallback_chunk_size(x.max(1MiB))` ≡ const 1 MiB — the sinks' `chunk_bytes` param is now inert and misleading; root cause is a TCP transport parameter (`chunk_bytes`) embedded in the transport-agnostic `PlannedPayloads` (planner payload composition itself is sound). Owner direction: slice 2 re-scope deferred until after a repo-wide design-coherence review (plan doc next). Sentinel cleared, `REVIEW.md` updated to verified (`[x]`).
DEVLOG.md:116:**2026-05-23 16:57:00Z** - **REVIEW**: Verification and acceptance of audit findings `audit-13`, `audit-14`, and `audit-15`. (1) Verified `audit-13-buffer-pool-double-locking` (commit `f9d3f2f`): single-lock return path and no-pre-zeroing optimization on `BufferPool` release/return routes are correct, performance validated, and dirty reuse contract pinned by test. (2) Verified `audit-14-resume-copy-redundant-seek` (commit `b7f8177`): sequential source `seek` elimination and destination cursor tracking successfully optimize syscall count during block-level resume checks; verified against existing resume test suites. (3) Verified and accepted resolution for `audit-15-grpc-missing-connection-timeouts` (commit `f0ed9e5`): accepted the recommend-defer decision rejecting global `Server::timeout` due to stream abortion risk, confirming HTTP/2 keepalive reaps dead peers safely. Sentinels cleared, `REVIEW.md` and `TODO.md` updated to verified (`[x]`).
DEVLOG.md:120:**2026-05-14 14:00:00Z** - **FIX**: post-edc11aa review followups. Two findings: **#1 Medium (delegated_pull logs failures as ok).** `service/core.rs:291` used `ok = !cancelled`, but `handle_delegated_pull` returned `()` and converted `run_delegated_pull` failures into an error-progress message on `tx`. So a real handler failure with the client still connected logged `delegated_pull ok ... errors=N` with `N` unchanged — the operator-facing metric contradicted the actual RPC result. Fix: `handle_delegated_pull` now returns `bool` (true=success, false=failure). The phased error is still surfaced on `tx` as before; the bool is purely the success bit. Caller in `service/core.rs` consumes via `tokio::select!` into `Option<bool>` — `None` = client cancelled, `Some(true)` = handler ok, `Some(false)` = handler failed. `ok = matches!(outcome, Some(true))`; `inc_error` fires only on `Some(false)` (handler failure), not on client cancellation. New regression test `handle_delegated_pull_returns_false_on_handler_failure` drives a blank-host source locator to trip `DelegationRejected` and asserts `false` is returned — pins the return-bool contract without mocking the full pipeline. **#2 Low (release-plan inconsistency).** Top-tracker said §3.1/§3.2/§3.3 closed, but §6 commit-sequence table still labeled steps 9–11 "Not started," step 1 said D4/D5/D6 still open, step 13 still claimed tagging is blocked on "product-owner ack of P1 deferrals," and §7 heading still said D4/D5/D6 are open. All four sites rewritten to reflect 2026-05-13 reality: steps 9–11 carry closing commits (`0d76c4f`, `6e750b9`, `6e750b9`); step 1 says "all taken — D5 modified by owner"; step 13 shows tagging blocked on step 2 only; §7 heading says "all D1–D9 decided, nothing owed anymore" with the section name retained for historical reference. **Tests**: `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean; `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+1 delegated_pull regression test).
DEVLOG.md:122:**2026-05-14 12:00:00Z** - **FIX**: post-6e750b9 review followups against the --metrics work. Four findings: **#1 Medium (active-gauge race).** `format_completion_line` was reading `active_transfers` while the just-finished RPC's `_guard` was still in scope, so a single completed transfer logged `active=1` — misleading for an end-of-RPC summary. Fix: rename `let _guard = guard;` to `let guard = guard;` and `drop(guard);` explicitly before the `log_completion` call in all three spawn closures (push / pull / pull_sync at `service/core.rs`). Now `active=N` reflects state AFTER the just-finished RPC is removed from the gauge. **#2 Medium (missing RPC coverage).** `delegated_pull` increments `pull_ops` + the active gauge inside `run_delegated_pull` (`delegated_pull.rs:227`) but never emitted its own completion line — the field would only become visible on a later push/pull log. Same for `purge`. Fix: (a) wrapped the delegated_pull spawn closure in core.rs with a start-time capture and a `log_completion("delegated_pull", ...)` call after the cancellation `select!`; `ok = !cancelled` per the cancellation-race semantics. (b) Extracted `BlitService::purge_inner` so the trait `purge` method can wrap a single call site with start-time capture + `log_completion("purge", ...)` + `inc_error` on `Err`. Both paths now contribute to `--metrics` per-RPC summary symmetrically. **#3 Medium (rustfmt clean).** Already clean on `HEAD` (the prior `cargo fmt --all` at the tail of `6e750b9` had landed); reviewer was working off an older snapshot. No action. **#4 Low (stale scope text).** Three sites still claimed `--metrics` was dormant / scaffolding-only after the §3.1 closure: (a) `crates/blit-daemon/src/main.rs:82` comment block rewritten to reference `log_completion` and call out the new operator-facing summary lines; the `--metrics` `eprintln!` info line now says "per-RPC summary lines on stderr." (b) `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md:500` — old "No reader / dormant" analysis paragraph quoted as historical context inside a "Resolved 2026-05-13" callout, so the section reads consistently as closed without losing the audit-time reasoning. (c) `TODO.md:248` — AI telemetry line struck through with explicit `Removed from scope 2026-05-13` callout pointing at §5.4 + deletion of the doc; predictor note added to clarify perf-history collection is unaffected. **New regression test:** `completion_line_reflects_active_after_guard_drop` constructs an in-flight transfer + a finishing transfer, drops the finishing guard, and asserts `active=1` in the formatted line — pins the call-site contract that the formatter sees post-drop state. **Verified:** `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean, `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+1 new metrics test, 9 total in `metrics::tests`).
DEVLOG.md:124:**2026-05-13 18:30:00Z** - **FEAT + SCOPE**: §3.1 / §3.3 / §5.2 / §5.4 closed (D5 modified, D6 default taken, BlitAuth + AI telemetry removed). Three owner decisions landed in one commit set: **(1) §3.1 D5 modified.** Rather than ship `--metrics` as dormant scaffolding, the daemon now emits a one-line stderr summary at end of each push/pull/pull_sync RPC when `--metrics` is on. Format: `[metrics] push ok in 1.23s (push_ops=N pull_ops=N purge_ops=N active=N errors=N)` — rsync/rclone-style operator visibility under systemd / foreground without needing the TUI. `crates/blit-daemon/src/metrics.rs` gains `log_completion(op_kind, duration, ok)` (no-op when disabled — atomics never loaded) and `format_completion_line` (extracted so the format contract is unit-testable without stderr capture). 3 new unit tests in `metrics::tests` pin the line format and error tagging. Counters themselves stay in place and feed future TUI work; the change just gives `--metrics` a real consumer for 0.1.0. **(2) §3.3 D6 default taken.** Phase 4.8 daemon FS-capability work (4.8.2 startup/idle daemon probes + 4.8.3 `blit diagnostics profile` capability probes) explicitly deferred to 0.2.0. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.8 now carries a scope-note callout marking 4.8.2 and 4.8.3 as deferred; 4.8.1 client-side `fs_capability` cache ships in 0.1.0 as today. **(3) §5.2 BlitAuth removed.** `service BlitAuth { Authenticate(AuthRequest) returns (AuthResponse) }` plus the two messages stripped from `proto/blit.proto`. `RemoteSourceLocator.delegated_credential` (forward-compat auth passthrough hook for delegated pull) also removed — field 10 reserved against future reuse. `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs` no longer sets the field. `DAEMON_CONFIG.md` Trust Model section rewritten to say "no auth, intentional, out of scope — terminate auth at the network edge" (was "no auth in 0.1.0; passthrough field exists for forward-compat"). `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md` got a top-of-file auth note flagging body references to BlitAuth/`delegated_credential` as obsolete (body retained as historical design record). **(4) §5.4 AI telemetry removed.** `docs/plan/AI_TELEMETRY_ANALYSIS.md` deleted entirely. References in `docs/plan/README.md`, `docs/plan/PROJECT_STATE_ASSESSMENT.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md` dropped. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.9 marked as removed from scope. Predictor (§2.8) is unaffected — performance history is still collected; no "analyze my history" feature is planned. **Release-plan tracker:** §3.1 / §3.2 / §3.3 all ✅ closed. §5.2 / §5.4 changed from "deferred" to "removed." `--metrics` decision (D5) modified from default; all other D-decisions taken at their plan defaults. **Net release-blocker count:** §2.6 (hardware-bound 10 GbE benchmark capture) is the last open P0. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+3 metrics tests); `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --check` clean.
DEVLOG.md:126:**2026-05-13 17:30:00Z** - **FEAT**: §3.2 mDNS TXT enrichment closed (D4 default taken). `crates/blit-core/src/mdns.rs::advertise` now publishes two new TXT keys: `module_count` (always set; the authoritative module count, distinct from `modules` which gets truncated past the ~180-byte TXT cap when a daemon exports many modules) and `delegation_enabled` (`1`/`0`; whether the daemon accepts `DelegatedPull` requests). `AdvertiseOptions` gained a `delegation_enabled: bool` field; the daemon at `crates/blit-daemon/src/main.rs:65` passes `delegation.allow_delegated_pull` through so the advertised value tracks the runtime gate. `MdnsDiscoveredService` gained `.module_count() -> Option<u32>` and `.delegation_enabled() -> Option<bool>` accessors — both return `None` for pre-§3.2 daemons so back-compat is graceful. `blit scan` surfaces both: JSON output adds `module_count` and `delegation_enabled` fields (omitted when absent via `skip_serializing_if`); text output shows `Modules (N): ...` when the names list is truncated relative to the count, `Modules: N (names not advertised)` when only the count came through, and `Delegation: accepts DelegatedPull` when the daemon opts in. 5 new unit tests in `mdns::accessor_tests` pin the wire-shape contract (truthy parsing, absent-field None, modules-accessor non-regression). Verified: `cargo test --workspace` all pass, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean, `cargo fmt --check` clean. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` +5 (was N, now N+5).
DEVLOG.md:128:**2026-05-13 17:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: §2.2 + §4 cleanup followup — `docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md` slipped through the original sweep (`aac13bf` + `8d43e4d`). Seven unconditional `blit-utils` references presented as live plan items: workspace-init step (line 82), Phase 1 layout (line 181), "Discovery & Admin Utilities (`blit-utils`)" feature section (line 337), Phase 4 tooling item (line 370), packaging step (line 399), acceptance-checklist entries (lines 410, 412). `docs/plan/README.md` labels this file as "Active architectural plan," so a new agent landing on it could reasonably infer the `blit-utils` artifact still ships. Added a post-phase implementation-note callout right below the header — same pattern as `WORKFLOW_PHASE_3.md` / `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` — pointing at `RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` §1/§2.2 and explaining that the as-shipped surface is `blit <subcommand>` from `crates/blit-cli`. Body kept verbatim per §4 closing-action style ("retained as design rationale"). **Tests**: not rerun — docs only.
DEVLOG.md:132:**2026-05-07 14:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup #2 catching review of `8d43e4d` — the RELEASE_PLAN_v2 itself was still presenting closed P0s as open work, which was a serious risk because `docs/plan/README.md` points new agents at it as the live source of truth for what remains before shipping. **Medium**: added a top-of-file "Closure tracker (last updated 2026-05-07)" block with a one-row-per-section table showing closing commits for §2.1/§2.2/§2.3/§2.4/§2.5/§2.7/§2.8 and §4, plus pending markers for §2.6 (hardware-bound benchmark — last open P0) and §3.1/§3.2/§3.3 (P1, doc-only). Each P0 section now has its own per-section `**Status (2026-05-07):** ✅ Closed <commit>` block at the top so a reader landing on a specific section sees current state without scrolling back to the top. §2.5 line 265 also still showed the old `blit completions remote <prefix>` syntax in the recommendation paragraph — corrected to the actual `<REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` form, matching the existing line-21 manpage. §6 commit-sequence rewritten as a status table with closing commits per row (P0 done except step 2 §2.6; P1 deferrable). §7 decisions table flipped from "Default recommendation" / "Owner" columns to "Default" / "Outcome" with closing commits where taken — D1/D2/D3/D7/D8/D9 ✅ taken, D4/D5/D6 still open. Test totals updated from the 2026-05-04 baseline (383) to current (407, after R41-R45 review-fix coverage). Footer "Last updated" + "Next review" rewritten. **Low**: `docs/plan/REMOTE_TRANSFER_PARITY.md:83` Success Criteria still said `blit-cli mirror -p -v local remote`; updated to `blit mirror`. Unlike POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md, this file is listed as still-applicable design in `docs/plan/README.md`, so a stale binary name there would mislead. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
DEVLOG.md:134:**2026-05-05 22:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup to the §2.2+§4 sweep — three doc misses caught by review of commit `aac13bf`. **Medium**: `docs/plan/BENCHMARK_10GBE_PLAN.md` lines 36 + 99-100 still pointed users at `./target/release/blit-cli` and `/tmp/blit-cli` for the active 10 GbE benchmark playbook (the plan that's currently driving the §2.6 capture). Replaced with `blit` paths. **Medium**: `docs/WHITEPAPER.md:738-739` build/run example used the same stale `target/release/blit-cli`; rewritten. WHITEPAPER:7 also still described `blit-cli` as the "user binary" without noting the `[[bin]] name = "blit"` rename + admin-verb merge — corrected. **Low**: `README.md:149` documented the daemon-backed completion fallback as `blit completions remote <prefix>`, but the actual CLI signature is `blit completions remote <REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` (matching `docs/cli/blit.1.md:21`). Rewrote the README line to show the correct positional `<REMOTE>` plus `--prefix` flag form. Two intentionally-left-alone files: `docs/plan/POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md:85` (real-world incident report dated 2026-05-01 capturing the exact pre-rename command that triggered the bug — historical evidence) and `docs/bugs/{single-file-source-silent-noop,copy-destination-semantics}.md` (bug reports also dated against the pre-rename binary). Verified by `grep -rn 'release/blit-cli\|/tmp/blit-cli\|--bin blit-cli'` across `docs/`, `scripts/`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` — only the three intentional historical references remain. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
DEVLOG.md:136:**2026-05-05 21:30:00Z** - **DOCS**: §2.2 + §4 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — closed the last P0 by removing/contextualising every `blit-utils` doc reference, since the admin verbs ship as subcommands of the single `blit` binary. **Per the §4 table**: (a) `docs/plan/PROJECT_STATE_ASSESSMENT.md` — added top-level "Superseded" callout pointing at the release plan; CLI Surface and Documentation sections rewritten so the `blit-utils` line is gone; Architecture Overview ASCII diagram now lists `blit` (admin verbs included) alongside `blit-daemon` only. (b) `docs/plan/README.md` — Current Status block flipped to point at the release plan as live source of truth; document index labels PSA as superseded snapshot, calls out BLIT_UTILS_PLAN as design-rationale-only. (c) `docs/plan/MASTER_WORKFLOW.md` — Feature-Completeness Goals, Phase 3 gates, and Telemetry row reword "blit-utils" → "blit" admin subcommands. (d) `docs/plan/BLIT_UTILS_PLAN.md` — added prominent Superseded header at top stating the `blit-utils` artifact never shipped and verbs live as `blit <verb>`; document body retained as design rationale per plan §4. (e) `docs/plan/WORKFLOW_PHASE_3.md` and (f) `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` — added post-phase notes at the top so historical body text describing `blit-utils` stays as workflow record but is not read as current state. (g) `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` — User Layer ASCII diagram redrawn (no blit-utils column), `### blit-cli (produces the blit binary)` rewritten with the merged structure including all admin verb modules, `### blit-utils` section replaced with `### Admin verbs` cross-referencing BLIT_UTILS_PLAN as superseded for artifact shape. (h) `docs/cli/blit.1.md` — SYNOPSIS adds `list-modules`, `ls`, `completions shell <SHELL>`, `completions remote <REMOTE>`, and `profile`; Admin Commands section documents all of them plus the `list` smart-dispatch wording matching §2.3 implementation. (i) `README.md` — annotated `crates/blit-cli/` repo-structure entry to clarify it produces the `blit` binary with admin verbs included. **Drive-bys outside the §4 table**: `docs/plan/AI_TELEMETRY_ANALYSIS.md` Phase 3 ("blit-utils Integration") section retitled and rewritten — the standalone-utils-binary integration path is moot post-merge; `docs/plan/TUI_DESIGN.md` ScanResponse-effort estimate updated to point at `blit-cli`'s scan parsing (was "blit-utils-merged"). Files explicitly **left alone**: `TODO.md` historical completed-task lines, `CHANGELOG.md` (already correctly says "merged into blit"), `docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md` (architectural plan, treated as historical), the GPT/audit review artifacts under `docs/`, and the `RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` itself (the source-of-truth describing the cleanup). Verified by `grep -rln blit-utils docs/ README.md TODO.md CHANGELOG.md` — every remaining hit is either an explicit "no separate blit-utils" callout, a historical TODO completed line, the CHANGELOG merge note, or the design-rationale BLIT_UTILS_PLAN now headed by its Superseded banner. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
DEVLOG.md:138:**2026-05-05 20:30:00Z** - **FIX**: Round 45 review of commit `f83a208`. **R45-F1 (High)**: while plumbing scanned features I left a `let total_bytes = scanned_bytes` alias in `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs:480` and the streaming summary at `:567` then read that local for `summary.total_bytes`. Net effect on the wire: `summary.total_bytes` (contracted as "bytes the pipeline wrote") was reporting scanned bytes, so any incremental skip-unchanged run with all-up-to-date files would have reported the full source size as bytes-written and overcounted throughput. Fix: removed the alias; streaming summary now reads `pipeline_outcome.bytes_written` from `SinkOutcome` directly. The verbose "Planning enumerated" line correctly references `scanned_bytes`. **R45-F2 (Low)**: planner + transfer sub-predictions were still passing `(all_headers.len(), total_bytes)` (which were aliases of scanned_* but only by accident) while the total prediction passed `(scanned_files, scanned_bytes)` explicitly. Made all three predictor calls share the explicit `(scanned_files, scanned_bytes)` feature vector so a future maintainer editing one branch can't reintroduce drift by missing another. New regression test `incremental_run_total_bytes_excludes_skipped_files` in `orchestrator::orchestrator::async_runtime_tests` runs the full local mirror pipeline twice (mirror=true forces streaming planner; skip_unchanged=true means run 2 writes 0 bytes) and asserts `summary.total_bytes == 0` on the second run. Verified the test catches the bug by reintroducing the alias — assertion fires with `total_bytes=4096, scanned_bytes=4096` matching the pre-R45 state. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (was 406, +1 new R45 regression test).
DEVLOG.md:140:**2026-05-05 19:30:00Z** - **FIX**: Round 44 review of commit `da6ced2`. **R44-F1 (Medium)**: predictor train/query feature mismatch. The pre-pipeline query in `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs` used `(all_headers.len(), total_bytes)` (scan-side workload), but `record_performance_history` recorded `summary.copied_files` for `PerformanceRecord.file_count`. On any incremental run the predictor saw "predict 1000 files" then trained on "5 files actually copied" — estimates drifted badly. Fix: added `scanned_files: usize` and `scanned_bytes: u64` to `LocalMirrorSummary` (`crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/summary.rs`) with a load-bearing rustdoc explaining the scan-vs-copy split; populated at every summary construction site (streaming pipeline, NoWork, Tiny, Huge, JournalSkip, single-file copy); `record_performance_history` now reads `summary.scanned_{files,bytes}` for the record's feature fields. The bucket-shape counters (`tar_shard_*`, `raw_bundle_*`, `large_*`) still reflect actual writes — they feed `derive_local_plan_tuning` which heuristically sizes destination buckets from past apply behavior, not scan size. Extracted `build_performance_record` from `record_performance_history` so the contract is unit-testable without polluting the global JSONL. Two new tests in `orchestrator::history::tests`: `record_uses_scanned_features_not_copied` (would have failed pre-fix on a 1000-scanned/5-copied incremental) and `bucket_counts_still_reflect_actual_writes` (regression guard against accidentally rerouting bucket counters through scan stats). **R44-F2 (Low)**: `blit profile --json` shape was unasserted. The pre-fix commit message claimed top-level `"predictor": null` for the empty-state, but actual output emits `{"copy": null, "mirror": null}` (predictor file loads even when no profile yet has training data). Picked the structured shape — strictly more informative — and pinned it in `test_utils_profile_json` (`crates/blit-cli/tests/blit_utils.rs`) with assertions covering both empty and trained states. **R44 plan note**: §2.8 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` is correctly framed now as "predictor observability and training," not "adaptive planning behavior" — the predictor is queried, surfaced via `--verbose` and `blit profile --json`, and audit-able, but the Tiny fast-path still does not consult it for routing. Added a Status block to the plan section documenting this. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 406 pass / 0 fail (was 404, +2 history regression tests).
DEVLOG.md:142:**2026-05-05 18:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.8 phase 2 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — orchestrator and `blit profile` now actually query the predictor. Three load-bearing changes: (1) `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs` introduces a `plan_done = Instant::now()` checkpoint between the planner stage and the pipeline so `planner_duration_ms` and `transfer_duration_ms` are correctly attributed; pre-fix both fields fed `record_performance_history` got the same whole-run time, so v1 predictor training collapsed into a single regressor. (2) Just before kicking off `execute_sink_pipeline`, the orchestrator queries `predictor.predict(DurationKind::Total/Planner/Transfer, ...)` with the just-scanned `all_headers.len()` and `total_bytes`; populates `LocalMirrorSummary.predictor_estimate` (new field of type `PredictorEstimate { planner_ms, transfer_ms, total_ms, observations, fallback_depth }` in `summary.rs`). `--verbose` now emits two lines: a "Predictor estimate: ..." pre-run line and a "Predictor delta: ..." post-run line showing predicted-vs-actual percent error per phase. (3) `crates/blit-cli/src/profile.rs::run_profile` now calls `predictor.coefficients_for(mode-only, ...)` for both Copy and Mirror, surfaces the result as a coefficients block in human output (`alpha_ms_per_file`, `beta_ms_per_mb`, `gamma_ms` per planner/transfer pair) and as a `predictor.{copy,mirror}` JSON object under `--json`. Smoke test: `blit profile --json` returns the new `predictor` key (null when no v2 profile yet); `blit copy` --verbose under streaming-planner workloads will emit the new predictor lines once 5+ observations accumulate. Tiny fast-path extension (the planner-overhead-dominance signal in the plan) deferred to post-0.1.0 — extending the fast_path scan budget risks edge cases on memory-bounded large-tree workloads, and the predictor's now-load-bearing model is only freshly trained on v2 data; revisit once we have multi-week observability under the new schema. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 404 pass / 0 fail.
DEVLOG.md:144:**2026-05-05 18:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.8 phase 1 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — extended the predictor data model so its predictions can actually drive decisions. Schema bumped to v2; existing `PerformancePredictor::load` already drops state on version mismatch, so the bump is transparent. New types: `DurationCoefficients` (planner + transfer coefficient pair per profile), `DurationKind { Planner, Transfer, Total }`, `Prediction { predicted_ms, observations, fallback_depth }`. New API: `predict(kind, mode, src_fs, dst_fs, fast_path, skip_unchanged, checksum, file_count, total_bytes) -> Option<Prediction>` walks a fallback chain — exact key → drop fast_path → drop dest_fs → drop src_fs → mode-only — gated at each step by `MIN_OBSERVATIONS_FOR_CONFIDENCE = 5`. `predict_planner(record)` / `predict_transfer(record)` / `predict_total(record)` are convenience wrappers around `predict(...)`. `coefficients_for(...)` returns the same `DurationCoefficients` clone that `blit profile --json` will surface in phase 2. `observe(record)` now updates BOTH coefficient sets in lockstep (single shared observation counter) so `transfer_duration_ms` becomes a load-bearing input. Removed the old `predict_ms(record) -> f64` and `predict_planner_ms(...) -> Option<(f64, u64)>` — both pre-v2 entry points returned values without confidence gating. Eight new unit tests added: `predict_below_confidence_threshold_returns_none`, `predict_above_confidence_threshold_returns_value` (verifies `fallback_depth == 0`), `predictor_learns_planner_and_transfer_independently`, `predict_total_sums_planner_and_transfer`, `fallback_chain_drops_fast_path_then_dest_then_src`, `schema_version_mismatch_resets_state_on_load`. Existing tests rewritten to use the new API. Phase 2 (orchestrator wire + verbose surface + `blit profile --json` exposure + Tiny fast-path extension when planner-dominant) lands in the next commit. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 404 pass / 0 fail (was 399, +5 new predictor tests + 3 retained-with-new-API).
DEVLOG.md:146:**2026-05-05 17:00:00Z** - **FIX**: R43 follow-up to R42-F2. The R42 commit added a comment on `MultiStreamSender::finish` claiming it routed through `drain_pipeline_error`, but the body still hand-rolled the same match arms — comment lied about the factoring. Real fix: extracted `drain_pipeline_outcome(handle: JoinHandle<...>) -> Result<SinkOutcome>` as the underlying primitive (Ok-on-clean / wrap-on-err / panic-prefix-on-join). `drain_pipeline_error` now delegates to it for the failure cases and only adds the "Ok-but-channel-closed" race-diagnostic on top. `finish()` calls `drain_pipeline_outcome(handle).await?` directly. Added 3 new unit tests in `drain_pipeline_error_tests`: `drain_outcome_returns_value_on_clean_finish` (passes the SinkOutcome through), `drain_outcome_wraps_pipeline_error` (same "data plane pipeline failed" wrapping that `queue()` produces, regardless of which call site triggered it), `drain_outcome_surfaces_panic_message`. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 399 pass / 0 fail (was 396, +3).
DEVLOG.md:148:**2026-05-05 16:30:00Z** - **FIX**: Round 42 review of commit `96cbb10`. **R42-F1 (Medium)**: §1.1 sweep was incomplete — three sites still silently dropped metadata errors. (a) `crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/tar_safety.rs::write_extracted_file` mtime + permissions (used by both pull tar-shard receive and the daemon gRPC push fallback); (b) `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs::execute_single_file_copy` mtime — single-file copy fast-path was bypassing the warn-don't-silence policy. All three converted to `if let Err(e) = ... { log::warn!(...) }`. Final sweep confirms no `let _ = ` over `set_file_mtime` / `set_permissions` / `set_keepalive` / `.flush()` remains in production paths (the two leftovers — `diff_planner.rs::sync_mtimes` test helper and `win_fs.rs::clear_readonly_recursive` cleanup utility — are appropriately best-effort). **R42-F2 (Low)**: the new pipeline-streaming regression test only exercised `execute_sink_pipeline_streaming` directly, leaving `MultiStreamSender::queue`'s producer-side join-error-drain logic untested. Extracted the load-bearing match arms into a free `drain_pipeline_error(handle: JoinHandle<...>) -> eyre::Report` helper in `crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs`. `MultiStreamSender::queue` and `MultiStreamSender::finish` both now route their failure paths through it, so the wrapping reads consistently as "data plane pipeline failed: <cause>" or "data plane pipeline panicked: <join>" regardless of which branch noticed the failure first. Three new unit tests in a fresh `drain_pipeline_error_tests` mod: `drain_returns_pipeline_error_with_failed_prefix` (Ok(Err(_)) path), `drain_returns_panic_message_for_aborted_task` (Err(JoinError) panic path), `drain_diagnoses_clean_close_race` (Ok(Ok(_)) clean-close-race diagnostic — proves we don't silently swallow it). **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 396 pass / 0 fail (was 393, +3 drain-helper tests).
DEVLOG.md:150:**2026-05-05 16:00:00Z** - **FIX**: §2.7 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — closed `docs/plan/POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md` Round 1. **§1.1**: `let _ = file.flush().await` in `sink.rs::write_file_stream` now propagates as a `Status::internal`-style error via `with_context("flushing {dst}")?` — flush failure is a data-loss signal (user believes file is durable when it isn't). Six other "best-effort metadata" sites in `sink.rs` (block-complete mtime/perms, tar-shard mtime/perms, file-payload mtime, write_block_complete mtime/perms) plus three TCP socket tuning sites in `data_plane.rs` (`set_keepalive`, `set_send_buffer_size`, `set_recv_buffer_size`) plus two `local_worker.rs::single_file_mmap_copy` mtime sites converted from silent `let _ = ...` to `if let Err(e) = ... { log::warn!(...) }` so misconfigurations are visible without making them hard transfer errors. **§1.1b**: `MultiStreamSender::queue` in `crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs` no longer turns "tx.send failed" into the generic `"data plane pipeline closed unexpectedly"` string. `pipeline_handle` is now `Option<JoinHandle<...>>`; on send-failure, `take()` it, await, and surface the underlying error (`Ok(Err(e))` → `e.wrap_err("data plane pipeline failed")`, `Err(join)` → panic message). New regression test `pipeline_streaming_surfaces_underlying_sink_error` in `pipeline.rs::tests` uses a `FailingSink` to assert the underlying error message reaches the caller verbatim. **§1.2**: `TarShardExecutor` decision — kept with a thorough docstring explaining (a) gRPC-fallback streaming framing differs from TCP path's contiguous-buffer apply, requiring a `TarShardReceiver` trait extraction to unify, and (b) buffer-pool sizing benefits from real fallback throughput numbers. Deferred to post-0.1.0; tracked in `POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md` §1.2. **§1.3**: `WHITEPAPER.md` §3 "Data plane wire format" updated — `BLOCK_COMPLETE := 0x03 path_len:u32 path:bytes total_size:u64 mtime:i64 perms:u32` (was missing the trailing `mtime + perms` shipped in `a7d659f`); added an explanatory paragraph on why the metadata trails the terminator. `POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md` Round 1 status block flipped to "Closed 2026-05-05". **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 393 pass / 0 fail (was 392, +1 new pipeline-streaming regression test).
DEVLOG.md:152:**2026-05-05 15:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.5 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — `blit completions` now generates real shell-completion scripts via `clap_complete`, honoring the `README.md:33` "shell completions" promise. Reshaped `Completions(CompletionArgs)` into a subcommand group: `blit completions shell <SHELL>` (bash/zsh/fish/powershell/elvish) writes a clap_complete-generated script to stdout; `blit completions remote <REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` is the existing daemon-backed CompletePath RPC the generated shell scripts call internally for `server:/module/<Tab>` style remote-path completion. New dep `clap_complete = "4"` on `blit-cli`. Three lib unit tests added: `clap_definition_validates` (forces clap's `debug_assert`), `shell_completions_generate_for_bash` (script defines `_blit()` and references known verbs), `shell_completions_generate_for_each_supported_shell` (totality across `clap_complete::Shell`). New integration test `test_utils_completions_shell_bash_emits_script`. Existing tests `test_utils_completions`, `test_utils_completions_dirs_only`, `test_admin_completions_*` updated to call `blit completions remote <target>` (subcommand split). README.md `### Admin Utilities` has new install snippets for bash/zsh/fish; `find --pattern ".csv"` updated to `"*.csv"` to match the post-§2.4 glob semantics. CHANGELOG entry rewritten to describe the two completion subforms. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 392 pass / 0 fail (was 386, +3 cli tests + 2 list smart-dispatch from §2.3 + 1 shell-bash integration).
DEVLOG.md:154:**2026-05-05 14:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.3 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — `blit list` now smart-dispatches by target shape. Bare-host targets (`server`, `server:9031`, `[::1]:9031`) route to the `list-modules` RPC; targets with a module/path tail (`server:/module/`, `server:/module/sub/`) keep going through the `ls` directory listing. Implementation: factored `run_list_modules`'s body into `pub(crate) async fn list_modules_remote(remote, json) -> Result<()>` in `list_modules.rs`; `ls::list_remote_path` short-circuits to that helper when `remote.path == RemotePath::Discovery`. Both code paths reuse the same `--json` and human shapes, so `blit list-modules <host>` and `blit list <host>` produce identical output. Two new integration tests in `crates/blit-cli/tests/admin_verbs.rs`: `test_admin_list_smart_dispatch_bare_host` (bare host gets the "Modules on …" header), `test_admin_list_smart_dispatch_module_path_routes_to_ls` (module path stays as `ls`-style streamed entries; explicit regression guard against routing everything to list-modules). The existing `test_admin_list_modules`, `test_admin_list`, `test_admin_list_subdirectory` continue to pass. `docs/cli/blit.1.md:13` (`blit list <REMOTE>`) and `:75` already documented the smart-dispatch wording — no manpage change needed. **Tests**: `cargo test -p blit-cli --test admin_verbs test_admin_list` 5 pass.
DEVLOG.md:156:**2026-05-04 22:00:00Z** - **FIX**: Round 41 review (R41-F1, R41-F2, R41-F3) of commits `0ca489b` + `090f5cd`. **R41-F1 (High)**: binary-rename sweep was incomplete — five test files (`diagnostics_dump.rs`, `remote_tcp_fallback.rs`, `remote_remote.rs`, `remote_checksum_negotiation.rs`, `remote_pull_mirror.rs`) still constructed `bin_dir.join("blit-cli[.exe]")`, so `cargo test --workspace` on a clean target tree failed with `spawn: No such file or directory`; the previous "385 pass" report only held because stale `target/release/blit-cli` was still around. All five updated to `"blit" / "blit.exe"`. Cosmetic panic-message strings in `remote_resume.rs`, `remote_regression.rs`, `remote_parity.rs`, `remote_push_single_file.rs` swept for consistency. **R41-F2 (High)**: bench scripts still pointed at the old binary — `scripts/bench_remote_remote.sh` (BLIT default + comment), `scripts/bench_10gbe.sh` (BLIT path), `scripts/bench_local_mirror.sh` (build invocation `--bin blit-cli` and resulting BLIT_BIN path) all updated. **R41-F3 (Medium)**: `find --pattern` glob set `literal_separator(false)`, letting `*` cross `/`. With the basename fallback this caused over-matches like `foo*.csv` matching `foo/bar.csv`. Switched to `literal_separator(true)` (POSIX shell-glob semantics; `*` does NOT cross `/`); basename fallback still gives "find files at any depth" via `*.csv`. Added regression test `test_admin_find_glob_star_does_not_cross_path_separator`. CLI `FindArgs.pattern` rustdoc rewritten to spell out the no-cross-separator behavior and the basename fallback. **Tests**: clean `target/`, `cargo test --workspace` 386 pass / 0 fail (was 385, +1 new R41-F3 test).
DEVLOG.md:158:**2026-05-04 21:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.1 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — renamed the produced CLI binary from `blit-cli` to `blit`, matching every doc/manpage/README example. `crates/blit-cli/Cargo.toml` keeps the workspace-member name `blit-cli` and adds `[[bin]] name = "blit"` so only the produced binary renames; cargo `--package blit-cli` and `-p blit-cli` references stay valid. Test helpers (`tests/common/mod.rs::TestContext::new`, `tests/single_file_copy.rs::cli_bin`) walk the build dir and previously hardcoded `"blit-cli"` / `"blit-cli.exe"`; updated to `"blit"` / `"blit.exe"`. CI artifact path in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and the binary list in `scripts/build-release.sh` + `scripts/windows/build-release.ps1` flipped accordingly. Per-platform run/bench scripts (`scripts/{linux,macos,windows}/run-journal-fastpath.{sh,ps1}`, `run-remote-fallback.sh`, `bench-local-mirror.ps1`) now reference `target/.../release/blit{.exe}`. Build-instruction strings in scripts updated from `--bin blit-cli` to `--bin blit`. Stale `target/release/blit-cli` and `target/debug/blit-cli` artifacts removed to confirm nothing was silently relying on them. **Tests**: `cargo build --release -p blit-cli --bin blit` produces `target/release/blit`; `cargo test --workspace` 385 pass / 0 fail.
DEVLOG.md:160:**2026-05-04 21:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.4 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — `find --pattern` now glob-matches via `globset`, replacing pre-0.1.0 substring containment. Daemon-side `stream_find_entries` in `service/admin.rs` builds a `globset::GlobBuilder` matcher once with `case_insensitive(!case_sensitive)` and `literal_separator(false)`; matcher tested against both the entry's relative path and its file-name basename so `--pattern '*.csv'` finds nested files via the basename fallback even without `**/`. Invalid globs surface as `Status::invalid_argument` from the daemon and propagate to a non-zero CLI exit. CLI rustdoc on `FindArgs.pattern` updated to describe glob syntax. CHANGELOG: removed "find uses substring matching" known-limitation row, added a glob behavior note under Admin Utilities. Two new integration tests in `crates/blit-cli/tests/admin_verbs.rs`: `test_admin_find_glob_invalid_pattern_rejected` (malformed `[unterminated` rejected) and `test_admin_find_glob_nested_with_double_star` (`**/*.csv` finds top + deep entries). Updated existing `test_admin_find_with_pattern` and `test_utils_find` / `test_utils_find_json` to use glob patterns (`*.csv`, `*.md`, `*.log`) instead of substring fragments. New daemon dep: `globset = "0.4.18"`. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 385 pass / 0 fail (was 383, +2 new glob tests).
DEVLOG.md:162:**2026-05-04 19:55:00Z** - **FIX**: Closed F14 from `docs/reviews/codebase_review_2026-05-01.md`. Migrated `crates/blit-core/src/change_journal/snapshot.rs::macos::capture_snapshot` from `fsevent_sys::FSEventsGetCurrentEventId` (deprecated by upstream in favour of `objc2-core-services`) to `objc2_core_services::FSEventsGetCurrentEventId` under the `FSEvents` cargo feature. Same call shape (`unsafe { ... }`), same `u64` return — only the binding crate changed. `crates/blit-core/Cargo.toml` macOS-target dep flipped from `fsevent-sys = "5"` to `objc2-core-services = { version = "0.3", features = ["FSEvents"] }`. Both deprecation warnings on macOS builds are gone (`cargo build -p blit-core` is now warning-free apart from the pre-existing unused-variable in `fs_capability/macos.rs`). With F14 closed, **14 of 15 baseline findings** are closed; only F15 (deferred structured-logging epic) remains. **Validation**: `cargo check --workspace`; `cargo build -p blit-core` (no fsevent/deprecation warnings); `cargo test --workspace` (383 pass / 0 fail).
DEVLOG.md:164:**2026-05-03 01:25:00Z** - **DOCS/TOOLS**: Completed Phase 3 cleanup/harness items from `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md` except live benchmark execution. DEVLOG.md:166:**2026-05-03 01:05:00Z** - **FIX**: Closed Round 37 findings from `docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md`. **R37-F1**: `RemotePullClient::pull_sync_with_spec` now preserves a typed `PullSyncError::Negotiation` for source-side pull-sync refusals before negotiation completes, and the destination `DelegatedPull` handler maps that to `DelegatedPullError::NEGOTIATE` instead of generic `TRANSFER`. Added core wire test coverage for initial RPC rejection classification and remote→remote CLI integration coverage where a fake source rejects `pull_sync`; the CLI surfaces "source refused delegated pull" and relay counters stay zero. **R37-F2**: delegated `BytesProgress` is now treated as cumulative state and converted to deltas before feeding the CLI progress accumulator; duplicate cumulative updates are not double-counted. Proto comments now document the cumulative semantics. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo fmt -- --check`; `cargo test -p blit-core pull_sync_with_spec`; `cargo test -p blit-cli remote_remote_direct`; `cargo test -p blit-cli --test remote_remote`; `cargo test -p blit-daemon delegated_pull`; `cargo test --workspace` (all passed; existing macOS FSEvents deprecation/F14 warnings and the pre-existing macOS test unused-variable warning remain).
DEVLOG.md:168:**2026-05-03 00:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Implemented Phase 2 of `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md`. Remote→remote `copy`/`mirror`/`move` now dispatch to destination-side `DelegatedPull` by default, with `--relay-via-cli` as the only legacy relay escape hatch. Added `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs` to build the same `TransferOperationSpec` as pull-sync, call `DelegatedPull`, surface stale-daemon/gate/connect/transfer errors without silent fallback, and print delegated summaries. Added env-gated test instrumentation in `blit-core::remote::instrumentation` so integration tests can assert the CLI sent zero data-plane payload bytes and never constructed `RemoteTransferSource` on the direct path, while `--relay-via-cli` does both. Reworked `crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_remote.rs` to cover direct byte-path isolation, gate-rejection no-fallback, stale destination `Unimplemented` no-fallback, and explicit relay. Updated CLI docs/help for `--relay-via-cli`. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo check -p blit-cli`; `cargo test -p blit-cli remote_remote_direct`; `cargo test -p blit-cli --test remote_remote`; `cargo test -p blit-core pull_sync_with_spec`; `cargo test -p blit-daemon delegated_pull`; `cargo test --workspace` (all passed; existing macOS FSEvents deprecation/F14 warnings remain).
DEVLOG.md:170:**2026-05-02 23:45:00Z** - **DOCS**: Round 18 finding R18-F1 fix — synced TODO.md with closed baseline + followup findings (commit `767f8ee`).
