docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:421:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:500 — size decision (daemon pull-sync) — determine_remote_tuning at 500 (gRPC fallback), 550 (data plane), 687 (resume). stream_via_grpc passes tuning.chunk_bytes (16-64 MiB) into GrpcServerStreamingSink at 515 where the clamp makes it inert.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:424:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs:31 — constant definition (third pool regime) — TAR_BUFFER_SIZE = 4 MiB, TAR_BUFFER_POOL_SIZE = 8, BufferPool::new(.., None) at 650 — gRPC-fallback tar executor pool, unrelated to both the chunk_bytes-derived pools and BufferSizer; acquire_buffer at 658 is #[allow(dead_code)].
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:434:The retry concept has a nominal owner — blit_app::transfers::retry (is_retryable + run_with_retries), wired to --retry/--wait for exactly three CLI verbs in blit-cli/main.rs:55-69 — but its effectiveness is smeared across strata that don't cooperate. The classifier sees only std::io::Error in the eyre chain, while the gRPC layers (pull.rs, push client helpers, the whole delegated remote-to-remote path, daemon delegated_pull) flatten every tonic::Status into a bare string through at least four independently-written conversion idioms, so on those paths --retry is documented-by-TODO to silently do nothing (pull.rs:322-329, 780-788). A second, dead classifier in blit-core/src/errors.rs contradicts the live one on ConnectionRefused/UnexpectedEof/NotConnected and remains publicly exported. The three seed channel builders diverge on the one axis that matters here: blit-app/client.rs preserves the error chain (connect failures retryable), the core pull/push builders strip it (same failure fatal). Sharpest problem: the combination of unshipped audit-h3c slice 2, no client keepalive, and chain-stripping means the gRPC fallback path — used precisely when networks misbehave — can hang forever, and when it does fail, the user's --retry flag won't fire and nothing says why.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:439:- The gRPC fallback transfer path can hang forever: no stall watchdog (grpc_fallback.rs slice 2 pending), no client-side HTTP/2 keepalive on any client channel (keepalive exists only on the daemon server, per seed context), and tonic Streaming::message() has no deadline. The TCP paths all have StallGuard coverage; the fallback — the path you land on precisely when the network is troubled — has none.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:440:- Same failure, different retryability depending on route: a connection-refused daemon is classifier-visible (retryable) through blit-app/client.rs:44 (.with_context preserves the chain) but classifier-blind (fatal) through pull.rs:245 and push/client/mod.rs:313 (string-formatted). So `blit copy --retry 3` retries a refused delegated/remote-remote connect but not a refused direct push/pull connect. Configuration divergence between three independently-written builders, exactly the seed pattern.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:452:  - divergence: Direct contradictions kind-by-kind: ConnectionRefused, UnexpectedEof, NotConnected are Retryable in the live blit-app list (retry.rs:40-44) but explicitly Fatal in blit-core (errors.rs:108-113, 'could go either way - default to fatal'). Interrupted and WouldBlock are Retryable in blit-core (errors.rs:94-98) but absent (= fatal) in blit-app. Only TimedOut/ConnectionReset/ConnectionAborted/BrokenPipe agree. The dead copy also carries an unused attempts/should_retry budget model (errors.rs:66-74) that the live loop reimplements differently (attempt_no counter in run_with_retries).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:460:  - sites: /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/stall_guard.rs:75 (StallGuard read) wired at pull.rs:1764 and daemon push/data_plane.rs:841; /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/stall_guard.rs:139 (StallGuardWriter) wired at core transfer/data_plane.rs:68; /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/grpc_fallback.rs:102 (slice-1 frame cap only; watchdog pending)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:461:  - divergence: Not a copy-paste duplicate — single shared TRANSFER_STALL_TIMEOUT constant and shared adapters (good ownership). The divergence is coverage, not configuration: every TCP byte path is guarded, the gRPC fallback paths have NO stall detection at all (audit-h3c slice 2 unshipped), and the daemon accept/token phases use separate ad-hoc constants (daemon pull.rs:698-699).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:465:- `TRANSFER_STALL_TIMEOUT` = Duration::from_secs(30) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/stall_guard.rs:69) — SIMPLE: zero-tuning, owner-decided, idle-not-total semantics — good. But grpc_fallback.rs:73-80 itself declares hardcoded wall-clock constants a violation of the FAST principle and promises a progress-cadence-derived policy for slice 2; the 30s figure is exactly such a magic number, just a defensible one.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:467:- `--wait default` = 5 (seconds, fixed interval) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:283) — SIMPLE: fixed linear wait, no exponential backoff or jitter; user must tune it. Also duplicated as literal 'wait: 5' in test fixtures (blit-cli/src/transfers/mod.rs:706,755,818).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:472:- `GRPC_FALLBACK_CHUNK_BYTES` = 1 MiB (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/grpc_fallback.rs:102) — FAST: deliberately caps gRPC fallback frames far below the 16-64 MiB TCP tuning so a future watchdog can observe cadence — trades throughput on the fallback path for observability that doesn't exist yet (slice 2 unshipped), so today it's all cost, no watchdog.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:478:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs:27 — Canonical classifier: is_retryable(eyre::Report) — walks err.chain() looking for a std::io::Error whose kind is transient — Only mechanism in the workspace that decides retry vs fatal. Sees ONLY io::Error in the eyre chain; anything stripped to a String is invisible.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:480:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs:55 — run_with_retries: the only backoff loop — fixed (non-exponential) wait, eprintln notice at lines 69-73, sleep at line 73 — retries==0 means single attempt. Comment at lines 5-10 ties viability to transfer resumability.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:489:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/grpc_fallback.rs:102 — audit-h3c slice 1: GRPC_FALLBACK_CHUNK_BYTES = 1 MiB frame cap + recv_fallback_message chokepoint; slice 2 (progress watchdog + retryable TimedOut) NOT shipped — Module doc (lines 34-71) names in/out-of-scope receive sites. Today the gRPC fallback has NO stall detection: tonic Streaming::message() can hang forever (and per the seed context, no client-side HTTP/2 keepalive exists to break it).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:490:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:307 — CLASSIFIER-BLIND: pull() control/fallback receive maps tonic::Status -> eyre!(status.message().to_string()) at 307 and 332 — Explicit TODO(audit-h3c-2) at 322-329: this strip means is_retryable will not classify a stall/drop here and --retry/--wait will not fire.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:499:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/delegated_pull.rs:216 — Daemon-side strip: upstream tonic Status flattened to status.message().to_string() into proto error/progress events (216, 245) — Error structure is destroyed before it even crosses the wire back to the CLI.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:501:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-cli/src/jobs.rs:348 — Resilience fallback (not retry): jobs watch Subscribe stream error falls back to one final GetState reconciliation (348-362, helper at 370); optional wall-clock deadline at 180-181 — Flags at cli.rs:137-143 (interval_ms default 1000, timeout_secs default 0 = wait forever).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:507:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-tui/src/main.rs:1457 — TUI site (recorded only): apply_deferred_refan re-attempts F2 watch setup after Failed when a daemon appears (1451-1462) — Event-driven re-attempt, no backoff, no classifier.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:514:No module owns error handling; ownership is smeared across all six crates plus the proto. The nominal owner, blit_core::errors (TransferError/ErrorCategory/categorize_io_error, errors.rs:12-121, exported at lib.rs:9), is dead code with zero consumers, while the live retry policy lives in blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs with an io::ErrorKind table that directly contradicts the dead one on three kinds (ConnectionRefused, UnexpectedEof, NotConnected). The workspace is uniformly eyre (thiserror and anyhow appear nowhere), but at least seven distinct styles coexist: chain-preserving context, chain-destroying eyre! stringification, three hand-rolled typed errors, tonic::Status-as-error with 116/189 constructors collapsed to Status::internal and line-dependent {err} vs {err:#} formatting, sentinel io::Errors for retry signaling, proto-carried string errors, and five different stderr prefixes. The sharpest problem is a self-documented broken contract: stall_guard.rs manufactures downcastable TimedOut errors precisely so --retry/--wait can classify stalls as retryable, but PullSyncError/format_status (pull.rs:82/116) and the dominant eyre!(status.message().to_string()) pattern (15+ sites) flatten errors to strings before the classifier sees them — the TODO at pull.rs:780-788 admits retry silently no-ops on the pull-sync path. Secondary: the same stringification drops the gRPC code, so a code-only Status renders as an empty user-facing error, violating RELIABLE's plain-failure principle.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:518:- --retry/--wait is silently broken on the pull-sync path: stall_guard manufactures io::ErrorKind::TimedOut precisely so retry.rs:27 can downcast it, but PullSyncError (pull.rs:82) and format_status (pull.rs:116) flatten the Status to a String before it reaches the eyre chain. The repo knows: TODO(audit-h3c-2) at pull.rs:780-788 documents the break, unfixed. RELIABLE violation — the feature degrades with no error at all.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:523:- Chain survival across the wire is line-dependent: ~48 daemon eyre→Status sites format with {err}/{} (outermost message only) vs ~9 with {err:#} (full chain), in the same files (pull_sync.rs:507 vs :653; util.rs:61 vs :110). Root causes of mid-transfer failures are intermittently amputated before the user sees them.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:530:  - sites: /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:116 (format_status: 'msg (code)', empty-message fallback to bare code); /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/helpers.rs:48 + pull.rs:307,332,493,507,541 + blit-app/src/admin/{rm.rs:30,ls.rs:74,list_modules.rs:32,jobs.rs:29,155,find.rs:55,61,du.rs:48,54,df.rs:33} + blit-cli/src/completions.rs:84 (eyre!(status.message().to_string()): code dropped, empty message → empty error); /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:698-751,844-908 + blit-app/src/admin/jobs.rs:83-118 (branch on status.code() with remediation hints); /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-tui/src/main.rs:5734 ('stream: {message}', code dropped)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:532:- [DIVERGE] Retryable-vs-fatal io::ErrorKind classification tables
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:534:  - divergence: Field-by-field: both agree TimedOut/ConnectionReset/ConnectionAborted/BrokenPipe are retryable and PermissionDenied/NotFound/InvalidData fatal. They DISAGREE on three kinds — ConnectionRefused, UnexpectedEof, NotConnected are Fatal in errors.rs:108-113 but retryable in retry.rs:41-44 — and errors.rs additionally counts Interrupted/WouldBlock retryable which retry.rs omits. Conflict is latent only because errors.rs is dead code (zero consumers).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:543:  - divergence: Approach agrees (drop the SendError, emit a fixed string) but the 30+ strings are ad-hoc per site ('data plane died' vs 'gRPC channel closed' vs 'failed to send ack' for the same underlying condition: receiver task exited), with no shared helper and no indication of WHY the channel closed.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:587:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/proto/blit.proto:811 — Wire error surface #3: TransferRecord.error_message — recents/audit history stores Status::message() string or "cancelled before outcome recorded" marker.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:670:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:165 — Producer: ManifestBatch = FULL enumerated server manifest (server_manifest.len() + summed bytes), sent before diff/need-list
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:703:- Keepalive asymmetry: server keepalive 30s/20s (daemon main.rs:138-139) but none of the three client channel builders sets keepalive, so client-side detection of a silently dead daemon relies on the data-plane stall guard; pure-gRPC-fallback transfers (no TCP data plane) have no liveness probe at all. The three builders agree today only via triplicated literals (pull.rs:241, push/client/mod.rs:309 inline; blit-app/client.rs:24 named) — the next edit will diverge them.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:728:  - sites: /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/data_plane.rs:15; /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/data_plane.rs:546; /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/grpc_fallback.rs:102; /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/tar_stream.rs:37
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:729:  - divergence: CONTROL_PLANE_CHUNK_SIZE, RECEIVE_CHUNK_SIZE, GRPC_FALLBACK_CHUNK_BYTES, TarConfig.chunk_size — all 1 MiB. grpc_fallback.rs documents the match as intentional ('Matches CONTROL_PLANE_CHUNK_SIZE by design') but they are four separate declarations; sink.rs:969-975 even codes a two-bound max/clamp shape as 'intentional defense if either constant changes later', acknowledging the non-shared definition.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:747:- `http2_keepalive interval/timeout (server only)` = 30s interval / 20s timeout (crates/blit-daemon/src/main.rs:138) — RELIABLE: clients have no counterpart, so a daemon that dies silently mid-control-stream is detected only by the data-plane stall guard or OS timeout; gRPC-fallback transfers (no TCP data plane) lean entirely on tonic defaults.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:751:- `GRPC_FALLBACK_CHUNK_BYTES` = 1 MiB (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/grpc_fallback.rs:102) — none — structural: documented observability ceiling (frame cadence for the stall watchdog) and keeps frames under the tonic 4 MiB default; this is the constant that makes PlannedPayloads.chunk_bytes inert on gRPC sinks.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:756:- `DEFAULT_PAYLOAD_PREFETCH` = 8 (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/payload.rs:108) — SIMPLE: fallback pipeline depth frozen; tuning.prefetch_count exists but only kicks in >=512 MiB and only the push client honors it.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:762:- `ENUM_BATCH_SIZE / MIN_BYTES_FOR_TUNING` = 500 files/batch; 16 MiB before data-plane start (crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:31) — RELIABLE: 500-entry batches are the plausible 4 MiB-decode-limit violator; SIMPLE: both frozen guesses.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:770:- `retry/wait CLI defaults` = retry=0, wait=5s, fixed spacing (run_with_retries, blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs:55) (crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:278) — SIMPLE: an explicit user knob in a zero-tuning tool (owner-approved robocopy parity); no backoff — fixed 5s spacing is a frozen recovery policy.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:779:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/auto_tune/mod.rs:116 — Sanctioned owner (real closed loop, LOCAL only): derive_local_plan_tuning — learns tar-shard/raw-bundle targets from perf history, clamped 4-128 MiB / 128-4096 files / 64-512 MiB — Only genuinely adaptive tuner in the codebase. Consumed by orchestrator.rs:494 via TUNING_WINDOW_SIZE=20 window. Remote transfers get no equivalent feedback.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:783:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:31 — Daemon streaming-pull gates: ENUM_BATCH_SIZE=500 entries/batch; MIN_BYTES_FOR_TUNING=16 MiB before negotiating the data plane (line 34, used at 236) — Frozen tuning. 500 entries × MAX_WIRE_PATH_LEN-sized paths can exceed tonic's default 4 MiB decode cap on the manifest stream (see risks).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:785:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs:124 — Push client pool sizing: pool_size=streams*2+4, buffer_size=chunk.max(64KiB), budget=buffer*pool*2; consumes tuning.initial_streams (503), negotiated.min(max_streams) (640), prefetch_count fallback initial_streams.max(1) (641-643), tcp_buffer_size (653) — The fullest consumer of TuningParams — push is the only path that uses all the fields.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:793:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/grpc_fallback.rs:102 — GRPC_FALLBACK_CHUNK_BYTES=1 MiB ceiling + clamp_fallback_chunk_size; structural (progress-observability rationale documented lines 85-101) — Deliberately matches CONTROL_PLANE_CHUNK_SIZE; keeps frames under tonic's 4 MiB default decode limit by design.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:808:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs:41 — Subscribe plumbing: broadcast 256, mpsc 64, DEFAULT_PROGRESS_TICK_MS=100 — Structural backpressure/progress cadence.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:810:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs:55 — run_with_retries — retry count/wait come from CLI flags (default retry=0, wait=5s per blit-cli/src/cli.rs:633-639); fixed (non-backoff) spacing — User-tunable knob (mild SIMPLE tension, owner-approved robocopy parity); constant wait rather than exponential backoff.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:826:- Windows parity is structurally untestable for the remote path: every remote integration test in crates/blit-cli/tests/ is #[cfg(unix)] (remote_parity.rs:8, remote_remote.rs:182, remote_regression.rs:22, remote_resume.rs:11, remote_tcp_fallback.rs:44, remote_pull_mirror.rs:44, remote_push_mirror_safety.rs:26, remote_push_single_file.rs:14, remote_transfer_edges.rs:9, remote_checksum_negotiation.rs:207, f2_chroot_containment.rs:22), so scripts/windows/run-blit-tests.ps1 (itself manual, not in CI) compiles them out and exercises zero remote-transfer behavior on Windows. AGENTS.md §5 'Windows parity matters' has no enforcement mechanism.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:827:- Mirror deletion behaves differently per transfer direction on Windows: orchestrator.rs:974-997 and daemon admin.rs:197-199 clear the read-only attribute before deleting; delegated_pull.rs:445-498 and blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:203-263 do not. A read-only destination file is deleted by local mirror and push mirror but errors (delete_listed_paths hard-fails the transfer; apply_delete_list's remove_dir failure is silently dropped at line 498) on pull mirror — same user command, three outcomes.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:831:- FilesystemCapability::fast_copy is a dead trait half with a second, strictly worse CopyFileExW implementation (fs_capability/windows.rs:230) and a Unix twin (unix.rs fast_copy); a future caller reaching for the 'capability abstraction' gets no block clone, no buffering heuristic, and no fallback — configuration divergence lying in wait, the exact gRPC-channel-builder pattern repeated for file copy.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:840:  - divergence: windows_copyfile: ReFS block-clone attempt first, adaptive COPY_FILE_NO_BUFFERING flag (memory-aware heuristic), fs::copy fallback on failure. try_copyfileex: COPYFILE_FLAGS(0), no clone, no NO_BUFFERING, no fallback, errors out. try_copyfileex is reachable only via FilesystemCapability::fast_copy which has zero callers (dead), but it is the obvious trap for the next caller of the trait.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:890:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/windows.rs:331 — Windows copy engine — windows_copyfile: ReFS block clone (FSCTL_DUPLICATE_EXTENTS_TO_FILE) → CopyFileExW with adaptive NO_BUFFERING → fs::copy fallback. Passes raw paths (no ensure_long_path); deep trees >260 chars fail unless OS-level LongPathsEnabled
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:895:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/mmap.rs:86 — parity fallback — cfg(not(unix)) mmap_copy_file is just std::fs::copy — no Windows fast path despite the name
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:899:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/fs_capability/windows.rs:230 — duplicate CopyFileExW (dead) — try_copyfileex: plain CopyFileExW with COPYFILE_FLAGS(0), no block clone, no NO_BUFFERING, no fallback — reachable only via FilesystemCapability::fast_copy, which has zero callers
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:905:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/path_posix.rs:36 — wire path-form owner — relative_path_to_posix: component-walk join with '/' — correct on Windows (splits on \) and POSIX (preserves literal backslash filenames); the documented canonical chokepoint
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:906:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/path_safety.rs:344 — Windows-form wire rejection — looks_like_windows_absolute rejects \\?\, \\.\, UNC, drive letters, single-leading-backslash on ALL platforms (line 85); good parity, but no reserved-device-name check
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:907:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs:974 — mirror delete, Windows-aware — clear_readonly_recursive before remove_file (974) and remove_dir (996) — the local mirror path handles Windows read-only files
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:909:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/tar_safety.rs:245 — wire permission application (tar shard) — cfg(unix)-only perms on extracted files; shared by pull tar receive and daemon gRPC push fallback
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:911:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/change_journal/snapshot.rs:157 — per-OS change detection — Windows USN journal (FSCTL_QUERY_USN_JOURNAL), macOS FSEvents, Linux inode/ctime — three parallel backends behind capture_snapshot/compare_snapshots; genuinely owned design
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:915:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/admin.rs:197 — daemon purge, Windows-aware — cfg(windows) clear_readonly_recursive before remove_file in purge_extraneous_entries (also used by push mirror via control.rs:347)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:916:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/admin.rs:618 — \\?\ prefix strip (inline copy) — strips \\?\ from fs::canonicalize output before sysinfo mount-point starts_with matching; misses \\?\UNC\ form
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:925:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_parity.rs:8 — unix-gated integration coverage — every remote integration test in blit-cli/tests is #[cfg(unix)] — remote_parity, remote_remote, remote_regression, remote_resume, remote_tcp_fallback, remote_pull_mirror, remote_push_mirror_safety, remote_push_single_file, remote_transfer_edges, remote_checksum_negotiation, f2_chroot_containment, plus local_move_semantics:120/238 — so run-blit-tests.ps1 exercises ZERO remote-transfer tests on Windows
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:943:- CancelJob authorization is IP-equality with loopback bypass (active_jobs.rs:1121-1132): a detached transfer started from a NAT'd or multi-homed client may be uncancellable by its own owner (Unauthorized), while any local process on the daemon host can cancel anything — both failure modes silent until attempted.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:962:  - sites: /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs:146 ('data plane worker cancelled'); /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/control.rs:313 ('data plane task cancelled'); /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs:805-807 ('cancelled via CancelJob' / 'client cancelled'); /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/active_jobs.rs:1065 ('cancelled before outcome recorded'); /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:823 ('enumeration cancelled')
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:980:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/active_jobs.rs:484 — token fire with peer authorization (audit-9) — cancel_authorized: IP-only match against owning peer; loopback and address-less (UDS) callers always allowed (cancel_peer_authorized at 1121-1132)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:982:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/active_jobs.rs:1065 — cancel fallback outcome string — guard dropped without record_outcome -> ring entry ok=false, error 'cancelled before outcome recorded' (mirrored in proto comments)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1016:blit-core is five recognizable strata stacked without demolition. (1) An Oct-2025 foundation layer: synchronous std::fs local-copy engine, test_-prefixed tests, aspirational '25GbE/rclone-inspired' docs, and vestiges of an even earlier 'Windows focus' restart (tar_stream.rs cites the deleted streaming_batch.rs). (2) An Oct/Nov-2025 remote v1 layer: two parallel hand-rolled gRPC monoliths (pull.rs 2212 lines, push/client 1133 lines) that duplicate each other's channel builder verbatim and tune via static byte-size ladders because the planned warmup probe was never built. (3) A small Jan-2026 design-doc restart (manifest.rs lived, errors.rs is dead on arrival). (4) An Apr/May-2026 pipeline unification (TransferSource/TransferSink/diff_planner) that is explicitly unfinished — its own docs say push/pull adopt it in 'step 3b and step 4', the unified FsTransferSource still calls push-era helpers, and re-export shims mark the move. (5) A May/Jun-2026 review-loop layer (~230 R##-F#/audit-* comment references) that added safety chokepoints (path_safety, path_posix, tar_safety, stall_guard) explicitly to replace 'half-a-dozen ad-hoc validators that drifted apart'. The local-copy and remote sides barely share idioms: sync+rayon vs async+tokio, NoopLogger vs ProgressEvent, four parallel comparison-mode enums with mapping shims in three crates. Dead weight is substantial: five entire modules (errors, tar_stream, zero_copy, delete, copy/parallel+stats) plus chunked_copy_file, the warmup machinery, transfer_payloads_via_control_plane, the deprecated Pull client path, and several fs_enum helpers have zero callers. Transport policy confirms the seed findings: chunk_bytes is a TCP knob threaded through transport-agnostic types and inert in the gRPC sinks (clamped to a 1 MiB constant), no max_decoding_message_size anywhere (4 MiB tonic default), and client channels set no HTTP/2 keepalive while the daemon server does — symptoms of the three independent channel builders. Searches run for auditability: per-symbol rg over crates/ and tests/ for every suspect type/function named above; module-path greps (mod::, pub use tracing); style greps (eyre/anyhow counts, finding-ID density, test_-prefix counts, box-drawing headers, #[tokio::test] counts); transport greps (keepalive, max_decoding_message_size, tcp_nodelay, connect_timeout); and git log --follow --diff-filter=A dating of every module cited.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1030:  - crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs:402-429 — single event loop carrying ~15 mutable locals (pending_queue, fallback_upload_complete_sent, need_list_received, data_plane_outstanding, need_lists_done, transfer_mode, ...)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1040:- **Pipeline-unification stratum (added 2026-04-07 to 2026-05-02): #[async_trait] Source/Sink traits, module docs citing docs/plan/PIPELINE_UNIFICATION.md with step numbers, descriptive (non-prefixed) test names, explicit 'this consolidates X that lived in Y' migration narration** — A deliberate re-architecture: TransferSource → diff_planner → prepare → TransferSink, used by local mirror, push data plane, pull TCP receive, AND the daemon's push receive. The migration is explicitly unfinished: diff_planner says push/pull adopt it in 'step 3b and step 4' which never fully landed — pull's gRPC-fallback control loop and push's NeedList event loop remain v1 monoliths, and the unified FsTransferSource reaches backwards into push-era helpers (layering inversion marking the seam). remote/push/data_plane.rs and remote/push/payload.rs survive only as one-line re-export shims to the new location.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1045:  - crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs:1093-1103 GrpcServerStreamingSink documented as "Mirror image of GrpcFallbackSink" — acknowledged structural duplicate (~200 lines each, sink.rs:951-1087 vs 1129-1240) rather than one generic sink
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1047:- **Review-loop/audit stratum (2026-05 onward): comments keyed to finding IDs (R##-F#, audit-*, 'Closes F7 of docs/reviews/...'), long forensic justification comments explaining the pre-fix bug, Unicode box-drawing test section headers (── R56-F1 ... ──), regression tests named after invariants, dated audit notes** — Not new features but a dense annotation/hardening layer applied on top of all earlier strata, plus new safety-chokepoint modules (path_safety, path_posix, tar_safety, stall_guard, grpc_fallback, operation_spec, instrumentation). Its signature is traceability: ~230 finding-ID references across 32 files. The chokepoint modules explicitly exist to replace ad-hoc duplicates from earlier strata ('replaces a half-dozen ad-hoc validators that drifted apart'), which is direct in-repo testimony of the multi-author drift problem.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1054:  - crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/grpc_fallback.rs:1 (added 2026-06-05) "audit-h3c slice 1" — module named after an audit slice
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1060:- Metadata-to-wire helpers: permissions_mode (identical body incl. #[cfg(not(unix))] `let _ = meta; 0` fallback), mtime-to-unix-seconds conversion (unix_seconds vs metadata_mtime_seconds, same negative-duration handling), and normalize_relative_path (identical three-line comment citing path_posix) — here: crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/helpers.rs:52-83 (normalize_relative_path, unix_seconds, permissions_mode); elsewhere: crates/blit-daemon/src/service/util.rs:127-158 (metadata_mtime_seconds, permissions_mode, normalize_relative_path)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1062:- Outbound gRPC control-plane payload streaming (FileManifest → FileData chunks → TarShardHeader/Chunk/Complete loop) — implemented twice inside blit-core itself; the payload.rs copy is dead, the sink.rs copy is live, and GrpcServerStreamingSink is a third 'mirror image' of the same loop for the pull direction — here: crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/payload.rs:234-345 (transfer_payloads_via_control_plane, no live caller) and crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs:951-1087 (GrpcFallbackSink::write_payload); elsewhere: crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs:1129-1240 (GrpcServerStreamingSink — same crate, documented at sink.rs:1093-1097 as the daemon-side mirror; the duplication is acknowledged, not abstracted)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1083:  - evidence: rg 'chunked_copy_file' across all crates: hits only copy/mod.rs:11 (re-export), copy/file_copy/mod.rs:7 (re-export), and chunked.rs:13 (definition). Not called even by copy_file's internal fallback (file_copy/mod.rs streams via BufReader/BufWriter directly).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1089:  - evidence: Self-documenting comment at payload.rs:247-249: "No live caller today (grep returns zero matches), but the function is `pub` and re-exported". My rg confirms: only the definition, the re-export at transfer/mod.rs:20, and a comment in grpc_fallback.rs:46. It duplicates GrpcFallbackSink::write_payload (sink.rs:951-1087).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1106:blit-daemon is four readable geological layers. (1) An original transfer-engine stratum (pull.rs, pull_sync.rs, push/*) with terse phase comments, per-file `eprintln!` hot-path logging under inconsistent bracket tags, and three hand-rolled stream-count ladders that re-derive (and then clamp against) blit-core's tuning. (2) A review-remediation stratum that patched R-/audit-/F-numbered fixes into those files four separate times instead of extracting shared code — the accept+token handshake exists in 4 copies with 3 sets of identical timeout constants (the `StdDuration`/`StdDuration2` aliases in pull_sync.rs are the smoking gun), and only the push copy sets TCP nodelay/keepalive. (3) A jobs/TUI milestone stratum (active_jobs.rs, core.rs Subscribe/GetState machinery, recents_store.rs, metrics.rs) written in slice-IDs (b-1..c-5b, m-jobs, rec) whose `#[allow(dead_code)] // lands in next slice` annotations were never swept after the consumers landed. (4) A newest delegation stratum (delegated_pull.rs, delegation_gate.rs, net_timeout.rs) with plan-doc headers, Step-numbered handlers, and a fourth error idiom (phased-progress-frames and String errors) on top of the crate's existing three (Status, eyre, and the select-outcome Option<bool>). Headline findings: the deprecated Pull RPC carries ~500 lines of unreachable TCP data plane that is ALSO the only multi-stream pull implementation — the production pull_sync path runs a single stream ('multi-stream support lives in pull.rs'), a direct FAST violation born of an unfinished migration; the push path drags a vestigial 262,144-slot manifest channel whose receiver exists only to drain it; cross-crate, the daemon re-implements blit-app's manifest enumerator and delete-list applier (the latter already caused one containment-divergence CVE-class bug, R58-F3), hand-rolls the FilterSpec conversion that blit-core validates properly, and inherits the keepalive-less, decode-limit-less client channel for its own outbound delegated connects while its server side carefully configures keepalive. Searches run: full reads of all 17 service-layer and support files (admin.rs/runtime.rs/active_jobs.rs/delegation_gate.rs skimmed at heads plus targeted ranges); rg by API (max_decoding_message_size, keepalive, tcp_nodelay, read_exact token), by symbol (.pull(, scan_remote_files, enumerate_local_manifest, metadata_mtime_seconds, permissions_mode, filter conversions via min_age_secs), by constant (32*1024*1024*1024 ladder), by annotation (allow(dead_code), cfg(test)), and by string ([data-plane], eprintln counts, 'deprecated').
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1115:  - crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:661-664, push/data_plane.rs:125,152-155 — `[pull-data-plane]`/`[data-plane]` bracket-tag eprintln throughput lines; main.rs:35,53 uses `[warn]`/`[info]` tags instead
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1134:  - crates/blit-daemon/src/net_timeout.rs:1-12 — `//! audit-1b ... Per the memory `feedback-server-await-timeouts`...` — doc comment citing an agent-memory artifact
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1154:  - evidence: control.rs:152-156 'Post-Phase-5, the TCP data plane receiver no longer consumes upload_tx (manifest metadata is now on the wire inline). Only the gRPC fallback path uses this queue. Tolerate a closed receiver' — but execute_grpc_fallback (data_plane.rs:590) actually takes `files_to_upload.clone()` (control.rs:275), not the channel. data_plane.rs:200-207: drain task `while guard.recv().await.is_some() {}` plus `let _ = cache; // headers come off the wire; cache no longer needed`.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1156:- TarShardExecutor::acquire_buffer (push/data_plane.rs:657-669), annotated #[allow(dead_code)] with the misleading comment 'Currently only used by the gRPC fallback path' — no call site exists.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1195:- **Three coexisting JSON-emission idioms: (a) #[derive(Serialize)] structs, (b) serde_json::json! ad-hoc maps, (c) hand-formatted JSON strings with manual character escaping** — JSON output shape is produced three different ways depending on which era wrote the module. The hand-escaped eprintln! NDJSON in the progress monitor is the riskiest: it does its own backslash/quote escaping instead of using serde, in the same crate that uses serde_json everywhere else.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1201:  - private clones: crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_tcp_fallback.rs:13-36 + :219 (DaemonConfig/pick_unused_port/ChildGuard), remote_pull_mirror.rs:13-36 + :218 + :247 spawn_daemon, remote_checksum_negotiation.rs:20-69 + :96 spawn_daemon_harness, remote_remote.rs:13-45 + :119 + :439 wait_for_port + :484 — each duplicates crates/blit-cli/tests/common/mod.rs:12-104
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1213:- Daemon-spawning integration-test harness (DaemonConfig/DaemonSection/ModuleSection serde structs, pick_unused_port, ChildGuard, readiness poll) — five copies inside this crate's tests alone; no shared workspace test-support crate exists — here: crates/blit-cli/tests/common/mod.rs:12-104 (the shared copy); elsewhere: crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_tcp_fallback.rs:13-36+219, remote_pull_mirror.rs:13-36+218, remote_checksum_negotiation.rs:20-69, remote_remote.rs:13-45+484 (four private clones of the same machinery)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1222:  - evidence: crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:133-138 declares the flag and its help text says 'until then this flag controls the GetState polling cadence'; crates/blit-cli/src/jobs.rs:171-173 admits 'preserved on the CLI for backward compatibility but has no effect under the streaming model'; `rg interval_ms crates/blit-cli/src` shows the only other occurrence is that comment — no read anywhere
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1225:  - evidence: File name + test_utils_* prefix (crates/blit-cli/tests/blit_utils.rs:11,38,161,402) date from commits 94ab3d2/2594ead before the crate's removal (d955600 'complete blit-utils removal'); coverage of list-modules/find/rm-directory duplicates admin_verbs.rs:9,187,463. Each duplicated test spawns its own daemon (TestContext::new at common/mod.rs:56 builds blit-daemon and boots it), so the overlap costs real wall-clock in CI
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1228:  - evidence: crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/local.rs:186-187 sets both fields; crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/options.rs:60-66 carries both with the R58-F7 comment; crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs:516 calls the bool 'Backward-compat'
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1240:  - evidence: crates/blit-cli/src/ls.rs:64-68 — the RemotePath::Discovery match arm bails with a message, but line 57-59 already returns for Discovery before the match; the comment admits 'Unreachable — handled above. Kept defensive'
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1250:  - crates/blit-app/src/lib.rs:13-14 ('Phase 5 A.0 status: scaffold landed; module-by-module moves are tracked in subsequent commits on phase5/blit-app-extract' — references a branch the repo's branchless rule has since outlawed)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1254:- **'audit-N' finding IDs plus agent-feedback memory names in doc comments ('audit-2', 'audit-2a round 2', 'audit-1c', 'audit-6 item 1', 'matching the feedback-server-await-timeouts principle').** — A later hardening wave by a different agent/era: bounded-connect work (client.rs), the retry/wait feature (retry.rs), and a test-backfill pass (filter.rs tests). Distinct idioms: pub const for the timeout, double-bounding (outer tokio::time::timeout + inner connect_timeout) with a long essay on why DNS isn't bounded by connect_timeout, and — uniquely in this crate — a direct eprintln! to the user from library code, violating the crate's own 'no stdout' contract stated in Cargo.toml and lib.rs.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1255:  - crates/blit-app/src/client.rs:3-11 ('audit-2: every BlitClient::connect(uri) ... matching the feedback-server-await-timeouts principle'), client.rs:30-36 ('audit-2a round 2')
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1258:- **Lettered micro-slice IDs (b-5, c-5a, c-5b, c-6, rec-2, m-jobs-3, d-25, d-68 R4) and gRPC-status-code → typed-outcome mapping enums.** — The jobs/observability era (admin/jobs.rs, detach flow in transfers/remote.rs, late endpoint-parsing fixes). Distinct error idiom: instead of bare eyre strings, status codes are matched into typed enums with remediation text (CancelJobOutcome, the daemon-too-old refusal on empty transfer_id). Streaming returned as raw tonic::Streaming<DaemonEvent> — a third streaming idiom alongside the du/find callback style and the consume-internally style of run_delegated_pull.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1290:- Stale provenance comments that point at code which no longer exists or contradict current behavior: (a) admin/ls.rs promises consolidation with blit_cli::util::metadata_mtime_seconds, but blit-cli/src/util.rs was deleted; (b) check.rs test asserts 'missing_on_src is populated by compare_trees regardless [of one_way]' while compare_trees only populates it when !one_way; (c) lib.rs and admin/mod.rs still describe the crate as a scaffold with moves 'tracked in subsequent commits on phase5/blit-app-extract' although all moves landed and the repo forbids agent branches.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1299:blit-tui (light pass, ~17k LOC across 24 src files + 5 screens) shows three clean strata, all post-dating the seed problems and all from a single project lineage rather than independent restarts: (1) the Phase 5 A.1 build-out (2026-05-17..22) — a 10,853-line main.rs that monopolizes async (38/39 tokio::spawn, all 34 #[tokio::test]) surrounded by pure sync state-machine modules sharing an identical 'monotonic generation-guard' idiom and saturated with d-NN review-finding comments; (2) a one-day audit-7d extraction wave (2026-05-23) of nine tiny pure-helper modules, each headed with the verbatim formula 'pure X extracted from main.rs (behavior-preserving — verbatim move, no logic change)'; (3) the Phase 6 dual-pane stratum (2026-06-05) with zero finding-ID comments and freshly re-invented size/mtime formatters. Architecturally the crate is well-layered against the seed problem class: it builds NO gRPC channels (tonic appears only as Status/Streaming types at main.rs:5688/5707; all RPC goes through blit_app::admin/transfers/scan) and shares config-dir resolution via blit_core::config::config_dir. The standout duplication is byte formatting: five private copies of format_bytes/format_size inside the crate (screens/f1.rs:614, f2.rs:562, f3.rs:586, f4.rs:699, dual_pane.rs:208), mutually divergent (only f2 got the TiB tier; dual_pane switched to 1 decimal), all shadowing the existing pub blit_app::display::format_bytes (display.rs:14). The Phase 6 dual-pane modules also re-do mtime formatting worse (raw epoch seconds at screens/dual_pane.rs:223 vs f3's YYYY-MM-DD at screens/f3.rs:598). No dead modules; the F3 single-pane browser stack (browse.rs + screens/f3.rs, ~2.6k lines) is the designated casualty of the queued Phase 6 rework and currently coexists with its replacement behind Screen::F3 vs Screen::Dual (main.rs:120-139).

docs/ux-feedback-migrate-games-poc.md:1:# UX feedback from the `migrate_games.sh` PoC
docs/ux-feedback-migrate-games-poc.md:33:This one change catches an entire class of future "silent no-op" bugs before anyone opens a ticket.
docs/ux-feedback-migrate-games-poc.md:162:Common-case flags (`-p`, `-v`, `--dry-run`) are interleaved with niche performance/debug flags (`--null`, `--force-grpc`, `--retries`). A new user reading top-to-bottom sees `--null` and `--force-grpc` before they see `--progress`.

.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit 84be1cc (run: git show 84be1cc). It implements ONE_TRANSFER_PATH slice otp-5a (plan docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md; finding .review/findings/otp-5-daemon-as-source.md): the daemon's one Transfer RPC now serves BOTH roles by dispatching on the client's declared initiator_role via a new run_responder — a DESTINATION initiator makes the daemon the SOURCE (pull-equivalent), streaming its module tree over the in-stream carrier; a SOURCE initiator keeps otp-4's push-equivalent. establish was split into exchange_hello + responder_finish; run_source/run_destination bodies were factored into drive_source/drive_destination; source_send_half's signature changed. A client run_pull_session was added. Check: correctness regressions (especially that the establish refactor and the drive_source/drive_destination factoring preserve otp-4's exact behavior — fault notify, peer_notified, data-plane prepare only for a DESTINATION responder, receiver_capacity advertisement), the acceptance criteria (roles chosen by declared role never a second code path; converge-up parity vs old pull), the invariants relevant to the touched area (byte-identical results, cancellation, byte-accounting, the same-build handshake, need-list strictness), and that the test count did not drop (1516 -> 1519). Confirm the guard proof holds (reverting the dispatch refuses a DESTINATION initiator). Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:42:I see the worktree already has an unrelated `Cargo.lock` modification and an untracked review result file. I’ll leave both alone and review the committed diff against the repo docs.
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:69:These outrank everything below. After a context compaction, re-read this block from AGENTS.md before continuing.
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:100:- Verify before claiming completion; the operative rules are in the Verification section below.
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:102:- Escalate an iterative process on stalled progress, never on duration. Each cycle must bank a verifiable delta — a test moving red→green, a finding closed with its guard proof, a build or type error resolved, a committed slice; a cycle that produces none is a stall. After a few consecutive stalled cycles (state the threshold you are using; default ~2-3), stop and surface to a human. A long run that banks a delta each cycle is healthy and must not be capped on duration or turn count.
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:115:1. Read `AGENTS.md`, `.agents/repo-guidance.md`, and `.agents/state.md` if present, plus relevant `.agents/` files, before making changes; note any untracked or ignored agent-control files that affect the task.
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:139:- `plan`: draft or update a durable plan before broad implementation work.
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:146:- For code changes, run the current automated verification before claiming completion.
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:154:- Never conclude a branch is merged from ancestry alone: `git branch --merged` can lie after an `-s ours` or octopus merge records ancestry without content. Verify the content actually arrived (`git diff <branch> <main>`) before deleting anything or treating work as landed.
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:155:- When working through a list of findings or fixes, address exactly one item per commit and commit each before starting the next. Batch sweeps spanning many findings happen only on the owner's explicit request. Whether work happens on a branch is this repo's policy, not this rule's.

.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:1194:| w5-1-log-backend | Medium | Install stderr log backend (warn) in all 4 binaries + one prefix convention; today every log::warn/error is discarded | `[x]` | master | `56bda09`+`7145202` |
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:1195:| w4-2-delete-push-upload-channel | Medium | Delete the 262,144-slot push upload channel (drain-and-discard; wedges gRPC-fallback pushes >262k files) | `[x]` | master | `03bcb1d` |
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:1198:| w9-5-jobs-lifecycle-e2e | Medium | jobs/detach lifecycle e2e tests (Subscribe, watch fallback, cancel exit codes) — net before W4.3 | `[x]` | master | `ad773d8` |
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:1205:| w2-2-stream-ladder-owner | Medium | Single stream-count/chunk owner: the 3 stream ladders died with REV4 (ue-r2-1e dial / -1f initial_stream_proposal takes file_count / -1h Pull RPC; absorption recorded D-2026-06-20-1); this slice closed the remaining leg — deleted the dead transfer_plan chunk lane (16/32 MiB ladder, Plan/PlannedPayloads wrappers, chunk_bytes_override + refresh sites, never-called plan_to_daemon_format, orphaned TuningParams); dial is the single chunk owner; W3.1's "settled tuning owner" = engine::TransferDial. Codex NEEDS FIXES (1 Low: new ensure_dial comment said "fallback batch" in the data-plane branch) → fixed `27f53a0` | `[x]` | master | `01209bc`+`27f53a0` |
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:1240:| design-3-unbounded-data-plane-connects | Medium | Both TCP data-plane connects lacked timeouts (audit-2 fix never reached the data plane); hung 60-127s on black-holed ports. Fixed: shared `socket::dial_data_plane` (bounded connect via DATA_PLANE_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT + w1-2 policy + bounded handshake write via DATA_PLANE_TOKEN_TIMEOUT; TimedOut in the chain → is_retryable transient); both sites collapsed (pull connect_pull_stream incl. resize-ADD, push connect_with_probe incl. elastic). +3 tests incl. deterministic stalled-handshake shape pin, mutation-verified; 1476→1479/0/2. Codex PASS (0 findings) | `[x]` | master | `49dcec6` |
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:1243:| w6-2c-daemon-progress-denominators | Medium | Daemon event stream has no denominators or file counts: TransferProgress hardcodes bytes_total/files_completed/files_total 0 (core.rs:240-242), TransferComplete.files 0 (:322-325, + tcp_fallback_used false :329), GetState bytes_total 0 (:994-996) — "N of M"/percent impossible for every consumer. Thread manifest totals + a files counter onto ActiveJobs rows. Filed by w6-2 verification | `[ ]` | — | — |
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:1244:| design-4-fallback-midmanifest-negotiation | High | Forced-gRPC pushes fail at ≥128 files (FILE_LIST_EARLY_FLUSH_ENTRIES; ~100 flaky). Mechanism VERIFIED two-sided: daemon announced fallback negotiation mid-manifest AND a force_grpc client streamed FileData with no negotiation at all — both racing the daemon manifest loop's FileData rejection. Fixed: daemon early-flush branch TCP-only; client gates fallback sends on fallback_negotiated. Owner-ratified 2026-06-12. NOTE: grade before design-5 (sequential overlapping commits on push/client/mod.rs) | `[x]` | master | `ddfeb58` |
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:1246:| audit-h1-mirror-relay-incomplete-scan | Data-loss | Reject `mirror --relay-via-cli` for remote→remote (round 2: gate moved before mirror confirm prompt + yes=false regression test) | `[x]` | `master` | `4467faf` |
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:1252:| audit-h3c-slice1-grpc-fallback-frame-contract | Robustness | Slice 1 of 2: gRPC fallback chunk cap at 1 MiB (`GRPC_FALLBACK_CHUNK_BYTES`) decoupled from TCP tuning; 3 CLI pull receive sites routed through `recv_fallback_message` (the chokepoint slice 2 will wrap with the dynamic progress watchdog). Round-2 adversarial concerns all addressed. Verified 2026-06-11 (owner accept; review assessment found the cap also fixes the tonic 4 MiB decode-limit failure — see DEVLOG, feeds slice-2 re-scope) | `[x]` | `master` | `bf4cc82` |

.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:1292:| audit-1a-delegation-port-zero | Robustness | Reject IANA-reserved source port 0 at the delegation gate before DNS/connect (audit-1 item 5; timeouts deferred to audit-1b + owner decision on idle-timeouts) | `[x]` | `phase5/a1` | `a3147b6` |
.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:1294:| audit-7-cargo-lock | Style | Track Cargo.lock for reproducible builds (4-binary workspace); remove from .gitignore (audit-7 item 10, owner-approved — supersedes the never-add rule for the lockfile only) | `[x]` | `phase5/a1` | `dfaecfe` |

.review/results/otp-5a-daemon-as-source.codex.md:1332:| bug-mirror-literal-backslash | Bug fix | Mirror failure on POSIX filenames containing literal `\` (e.g. macOS Logic Pro `1\4 Single.pst`): blanket `path.to_string_lossy().replace('\\', "/")` at 11 sites was destructive. New canonical `blit_core::path_posix::relative_path_to_posix` (component-walk) + every Path→wire site routed through it. Round 2: GPT-reopen — `relative_str_to_posix` was dropping trailing `/` in admin completion input (regressed dir-prefix completions); now preserves trailing-separator UX semantic for user input while keeping wire/manifest form clean. 10 regression tests. .review/resultelative_path 'twins' are thin wrappers, not duplication. MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES is single-sourced in tar_safety.rs and referenced (not re-typed) by pipeline.rs per the map; I did not re-verify every wire cap. Deliberately NOT reported (cross-referenced instead): the triplicated gRPC channel builder, client keepalive absence, tonic decode limit, and the Status->eyre stringification family (all inside queued slice-2 transport work, STATE.md Queue item 2); unbounded data-plane connects (design-3); the CLI pull byte double-count bug itself (design-1 — I filed only the structural folding-rule duplication around it); orphaned daemon data planes / AbortOnDrop-vs-bare-JoinHandle (design-2 territory). Dropped as low severity: tar-shard 1 MiB reservation duplicated twice; TUI byte-formatter ladder duplicated in f2.rs/f4.rs plus blit-app/display.rs (display-only; TUI light-pass rule); throughput smoothing triplication (per-layer cadences arguably legitimate); mpsc send-error fixed-string family (likely reshaped by queued error-chain work); the '1 MiB' five-constant family (its real risk is the decode-limit invariant, which is queued). Not covered in depth: blit-prometheus-bridge (map reports it self-consistent; spot-checked only), blit-tui internals beyond progress_accum (Phase 6 rule), the double-buffered send/receive loop twins in data_plane.rs (read but judged a FAST/design question for the adaptive-streams landing rather than a consolidation slice), and Windows casefold-key divergence (already tracked as h-paths-2 in docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-paths.md per the map; not re-filed).
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:10941:7590:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1171:Checked and re-derived from code this session: blit-core/src/errors.rs (full read), blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs (full read), blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs 75-135 (PullSyncError/format_status), push/client/helpers.rs (map_status, [push] prefix), all daemon service files via targeted rg + reads (Status constructor tally: 116 internal / ~199 total; {err} vs {err:#} counts 12 vs ~69), util.rs (full read), delegated_pull.rs 190-260 and 360-375, core.rs outcome_from_status and TransferError event construction, blit-cli/src/jobs.rs watch render paths, blit-app/src/admin/{rm,jobs}.rs, blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs 698-760, proto/blit.proto error messages, sink.rs log::warn and mpsc sites, logger.rs (full read), workspace-wide rg for log backends (none exist). Found clean: run_with_retries loop logic and its tests (correct budget/classification semantics); format_status itself (the one well-designed converter — finding is that it is private); admin/jobs.rs cancel_job code branching (good pattern, includes code+message); delegated_pull's R37-F1 negotiation-phase preservation via PullSyncError downcast (works as documented); color_eyre installed in blit-cli main; prometheus bridge stderr usage is internally consistent; copy-engine errors propagate correctly despite the noop Logger (verified Err(e) returned at file_copy/mod.rs:215-223). Not covered per instructions: deep TUI internals (only the main.rs:5734 boundary site, kept as light-pass evidence); the queued slice-2 territory — connect-site chain stripping (pull.rs:245, push/client/mod.rs:313), the TODO(audit-h3c-2) retry no-op at pull.rs:780-788, Status→eyre chain preservation, and retry-classifier extension to tonic codes — cross-referenced in findings 4 and 3 rather than re-reported; the three already-filed design-1/2/3 findings. Not exhaustively audited: blit-app TUI-facing transfer progress text, Windows-only win_fs error paths, and the bug-report-style empty-message reachability of every individual tonic transport error variant (mechanism shown via the in-repo format_status guard plus core.rs unwrap_or(\"\") instead).
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:10942:7641:docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:5:Blit ships a coherent, well-tested 0.1.0 surface (hybrid TCP + gRPC transport, unified receive pipeline, always-on F2 containment, delegated remote→remote with explicit-only fallback, robust per-RPC daemon RPCs), but the plan corpus has not kept up with what shipped. The single largest gap is **TUI**: the rework's "Pick-not-Type" dual-pane shell is the default screen yet has no transfer-execution wiring (action bar is render-only), and the design's `detach=true` for remote→remote TUI delegations is hardcoded `false` — both undermine the "single pane of glass survives initiator disconnect" promise. Lower-altitude but pervasive drift: the planner's documented "1 s heartbeat / 10 s stall detector" architecture was never built (we ship a synchronous orchestrator with a 30 s data-plane idle guard on pull only), `--detach` shipped despite being "out of scope" in REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN, the manpage omits eight transfer flags including data-loss-class ones (`--null`, `--detach`, `--delete-scope`, `--force`, `--ignore-existing`) and two whole verbs (`jobs`, `check`), and the architecture/whitepaper still narrate auth/TLS planning that was explicitly removed from scope. Cross-cutting inconsistencies cluster around five seams — path rendering (3 ad-hoc `Path→wire` helpers with different empty-path encodings), error wrapping (admin verbs strip Status codes while jobs verbs preserve them, breaking `is_retryable`), endpoint parse Err handling (TUI F1 confirm-detail silently classifies as Local, violating an explicit project-memory rule reopened 4×), timeout/keepalive symmetry (stall guard only on pull-receive, TCP keepalive only on push-receive accept, three independent `connect_with_timeout` implementations), and destructive-action prompts (CLI `perf --clear` fires silently while TUI confirms; no `--yes` parity for `jobs clear-recent`/`jobs cancel`). What's solid: the data-plane wire format, F2 containment chokepoint, delegation gate with DNS-rebinding mitigation, spec-version fail-closed, `pull_sync_with_spec` endpoint-isolation, BlitAuth removal, and the CLI's documented data-loss reject-gates on `move` are all faithfully implemented and tested.

.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:11812:- [crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs](/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs:394) — **Medium** — `Transfer` uses `resolve_streaming_outcome`; on `CancelJob` that helper emits `Status::cancelled` instead of a `SessionError{CANCELLED}` frame. The contract requires a framed cancel error, so clients see a transport error/Internal path instead. This is acknowledged in the finding doc as an otp-4b gap, but it is still a contract miss for this served RPC path.
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:11819:- [crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs](/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs:394) — **Medium** — `Transfer` uses `resolve_streaming_outcome`; on `CancelJob` that helper emits `Status::cancelled` instead of a `SessionError{CANCELLED}` frame. The contract requires a framed cancel error, so clients see a transport error/Internal path instead. This is acknowledged in the finding doc as an otp-4b gap, but it is still a contract miss for this served RPC path.
.review/results/otp-4b2-data-plane.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit dce56de (run: git show dce56de). It implements slice otp-4b-2 of docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md — porting the sf-2 mid-transfer stream resize + multi-stream growth onto the unified transfer session (spec: docs/TRANSFER_SESSION.md; the resize wire frames DataPlaneResize=16 / DataPlaneResizeAck=17 were frozen at otp-1, this slice only consumes them). The implementation record is .review/findings/otp-4b-data-plane.md (otp-4b-2 section). Focus: (1) correctness of the SOURCE resize driver (transfer_session/mod.rs source_send_half: shape proposal via TransferDial::propose_shape_resize, one-in-flight discipline, ResizeAck handling, resolve_in_flight_resize before finish) and DESTINATION accept loop (transfer_session/data_plane.rs accept_loop select, arming, ceiling bound, ResponderDataPlaneRun::finish termination); (2) deadlock/hang and orphaned-arm hazards, select-cancellation safety, task teardown (AbortOnDrop); (3) the byte-transfer invariants (byte-identical results, StallGuard, cancellation, byte-accounting); (4) that the resize-dial-fatal simplification vs old push's arm-TTL is sound and orphan-free; (5) whether the sf-2 pin (many_tiny_files_shape_correct_to_more_than_one_stream) actually guards the feature and the test count did not drop (1512 -> 1513). Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/otp-4b2-data-plane.codex.md:4482:    86	- Resize-dial failure is fatal (vs old push's arm-TTL non-fatal recovery)
.review/results/otp-4b2-data-plane.codex.md:4483:    87	  — deliberate simplification; a same-build LAN/loopback epoch-N dial to
.review/results/otp-4b2-data-plane.codex.md:4484:    88	  an already-accepting listener essentially never fails, and fatal
.review/results/otp-4b2-data-plane.codex.md:4509:The source driver has the intended one-in-flight structure: proposals are kept in `pending_resize`, acks are epoch-matched, and the final pending proposal is drained before data-plane finish. I’m now looking for edge cases around ack loss/refusal, control-channel close, and whether the test can pass while missing a byte-accounting or teardown regression.

.review/results/otp-4b2-data-plane.codex.md:5281:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:818:**Mechanism**: receive_fallback_data builds TarShardExecutor::new(MAX_PARALLEL_TAR_TASKS) (push/data_plane.rs:338) with the constant 4 declared at line 28; the semaphore at line 648 gates spawn_blocking untar jobs (line 695). Nothing keys this to core count, while local copy work uses rayon's core-count default (copy/parallel.rs:30) and CopyOptions defaults workers to num_cpus (orchestrator/options.rs:99) — the adaptation pattern exists in the same workspace. Note (correcting the design map's framing): this cap governs only the gRPC fallback receive; the TCP push path untars inline inside FsTransferSink with parallelism = N accepted streams (data_plane.rs:210-214 comment), so the impact is the fallback path's overlap of network receive with extraction on big-core servers, not a 32-stream bottleneck.
.review/results/otp-4b2-data-plane.codex.md:5282:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1151:  - kill reason: The headline blank-render impact is unreachable: all four production callers of build_transfer_finished_event pass Some(non-empty) on failure (core.rs:533/591/653 via outcome_from_status at core.rs:1330, which forwards status.message() on every Err; core.rs:801-818 uses hard-coded markers), so the unwrap_or("") at core.rs:336 fires only in tests (core.rs:1790-2037). No Status in blit-daemon is built with an empty message — every construction in util.rs, pull_sync.rs, pull.rs, and push/*.rs uses a literal or format!, and the one raw propagation (push/control.rs:62) yields descriptive tonic transport statuses. The proto's documented empty case (blit.proto:986-993) is the no-outcome drop path, where active_jobs.rs:1065 substitutes the non-empty "cancelled before outcome recorded" marker into recents and the event send at core.rs:541 is never reached, so nothing blank is ever rendered by jobs.rs:336 or blit-tui main.rs:5732-5735 (the latter is the subscribe-stream error path, not the TransferError render). The residual code-drop adds little to self-describing format! messages and overlaps the queued slice-2 tonic-Status error-chain work, which I was instructed to cross-reference rather than re-report.

.review/results/otp-4b2-data-plane.codex.md:5322:.review/results/otp-4b2-data-plane.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit dce56de (run: git show dce56de). It implements slice otp-4b-2 of docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md — porting the sf-2 mid-transfer stream resize + multi-stream growth onto the unified transfer session (spec: docs/TRANSFER_SESSION.md; the resize wire frames DataPlaneResize=16 / DataPlaneResizeAck=17 were frozen at otp-1, this slice only consumes them). The implementation record is .review/findings/otp-4b-data-plane.md (otp-4b-2 section). Focus: (1) correctness of the SOURCE resize driver (transfer_session/mod.rs source_send_half: shape proposal via TransferDial::propose_shape_resize, one-in-flight discipline, ResizeAck handling, resolve_in_flight_resize before finish) and DESTINATION accept loop (transfer_session/data_plane.rs accept_loop select, arming, ceiling bound, ResponderDataPlaneRun::finish termination); (2) deadlock/hang and orphaned-arm hazards, select-cancellation safety, task teardown (AbortOnDrop); (3) the byte-transfer invariants (byte-identical results, StallGuard, cancellation, byte-accounting); (4) that the resize-dial-fatal simplification vs old push's arm-TTL is sound and orphan-free; (5) whether the sf-2 pin (many_tiny_files_shape_correct_to_more_than_one_stream) actually guards the feature and the test count did not drop (1512 -> 1513). Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.
