Unlike POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md, this file is listed as still-applicable design in `docs/plan/README.md`, so a stale binary name there would mislead. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
DEVLOG.md:134:**2026-05-05 22:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup to the §2.2+§4 sweep — three doc misses caught by review of commit `aac13bf`. **Medium**: `docs/plan/BENCHMARK_10GBE_PLAN.md` lines 36 + 99-100 still pointed users at `./target/release/blit-cli` and `/tmp/blit-cli` for the active 10 GbE benchmark playbook (the plan that's currently driving the §2.6 capture). Replaced with `blit` paths. **Medium**: `docs/WHITEPAPER.md:738-739` build/run example used the same stale `target/release/blit-cli`; rewritten. WHITEPAPER:7 also still described `blit-cli` as the "user binary" without noting the `[[bin]] name = "blit"` rename + admin-verb merge — corrected. **Low**: `README.md:149` documented the daemon-backed completion fallback as `blit completions remote <prefix>`, but the actual CLI signature is `blit completions remote <REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` (matching `docs/cli/blit.1.md:21`). Rewrote the README line to show the correct positional `<REMOTE>` plus `--prefix` flag form. Two intentionally-left-alone files: `docs/plan/POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md:85` (real-world incident report dated 2026-05-01 capturing the exact pre-rename command that triggered the bug — historical evidence) and `docs/bugs/{single-file-source-silent-noop,copy-destination-semantics}.md` (bug reports also dated against the pre-rename binary). Verified by `grep -rn 'release/blit-cli\|/tmp/blit-cli\|--bin blit-cli'` across `docs/`, `scripts/`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` — only the three intentional historical references remain. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
DEVLOG.md:142:**2026-05-05 18:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.8 phase 2 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — orchestrator and `blit profile` now actually query the predictor. Three load-bearing changes: (1) `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs` introduces a `plan_done = Instant::now()` checkpoint between the planner stage and the pipeline so `planner_duration_ms` and `transfer_duration_ms` are correctly attributed; pre-fix both fields fed `record_performance_history` got the same whole-run time, so v1 predictor training collapsed into a single regressor. (2) Just before kicking off `execute_sink_pipeline`, the orchestrator queries `predictor.predict(DurationKind::Total/Planner/Transfer, ...)` with the just-scanned `all_headers.len()` and `total_bytes`; populates `LocalMirrorSummary.predictor_estimate` (new field of type `PredictorEstimate { planner_ms, transfer_ms, total_ms, observations, fallback_depth }` in `summary.rs`). `--verbose` now emits two lines: a "Predictor estimate: ..." pre-run line and a "Predictor delta: ..." post-run line showing predicted-vs-actual percent error per phase. (3) `crates/blit-cli/src/profile.rs::run_profile` now calls `predictor.coefficients_for(mode-only, ...)` for both Copy and Mirror, surfaces the result as a coefficients block in human output (`alpha_ms_per_file`, `beta_ms_per_mb`, `gamma_ms` per planner/transfer pair) and as a `predictor.{copy,mirror}` JSON object under `--json`. Smoke test: `blit profile --json` returns the new `predictor` key (null when no v2 profile yet); `blit copy` --verbose under streaming-planner workloads will emit the new predictor lines once 5+ observations accumulate. Tiny fast-path extension (the planner-overhead-dominance signal in the plan) deferred to post-0.1.0 — extending the fast_path scan budget risks edge cases on memory-bounded large-tree workloads, and the predictor's now-load-bearing model is only freshly trained on v2 data; revisit once we have multi-week observability under the new schema. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 404 pass / 0 fail.
DEVLOG.md:144:**2026-05-05 18:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.8 phase 1 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — extended the predictor data model so its predictions can actually drive decisions. Schema bumped to v2; existing `PerformancePredictor::load` already drops state on version mismatch, so the bump is transparent. New types: `DurationCoefficients` (planner + transfer coefficient pair per profile), `DurationKind { Planner, Transfer, Total }`, `Prediction { predicted_ms, observations, fallback_depth }`. New API: `predict(kind, mode, src_fs, dst_fs, fast_path, skip_unchanged, checksum, file_count, total_bytes) -> Option<Prediction>` walks a fallback chain — exact key → drop fast_path → drop dest_fs → drop src_fs → mode-only — gated at each step by `MIN_OBSERVATIONS_FOR_CONFIDENCE = 5`. `predict_planner(record)` / `predict_transfer(record)` / `predict_total(record)` are convenience wrappers around `predict(...)`. `coefficients_for(...)` returns the same `DurationCoefficients` clone that `blit profile --json` will surface in phase 2. `observe(record)` now updates BOTH coefficient sets in lockstep (single shared observation counter) so `transfer_duration_ms` becomes a load-bearing input. Removed the old `predict_ms(record) -> f64` and `predict_planner_ms(...) -> Option<(f64, u64)>` — both pre-v2 entry points returned values without confidence gating. Eight new unit tests added: `predict_below_confidence_threshold_returns_none`, `predict_above_confidence_threshold_returns_value` (verifies `fallback_depth == 0`), `predictor_learns_planner_and_transfer_independently`, `predict_total_sums_planner_and_transfer`, `fallback_chain_drops_fast_path_then_dest_then_src`, `schema_version_mismatch_resets_state_on_load`. Existing tests rewritten to use the new API. Phase 2 (orchestrator wire + verbose surface + `blit profile --json` exposure + Tiny fast-path extension when planner-dominant) lands in the next commit. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 404 pass / 0 fail (was 399, +5 new predictor tests + 3 retained-with-new-API).
DEVLOG.md:148:**2026-05-05 16:30:00Z** - **FIX**: Round 42 review of commit `96cbb10`. **R42-F1 (Medium)**: §1.1 sweep was incomplete — three sites still silently dropped metadata errors. (a) `crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/tar_safety.rs::write_extracted_file` mtime + permissions (used by both pull tar-shard receive and the daemon gRPC push fallback); (b) `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs::execute_single_file_copy` mtime — single-file copy fast-path was bypassing the warn-don't-silence policy. All three converted to `if let Err(e) = ... { log::warn!(...) }`. Final sweep confirms no `let _ = ` over `set_file_mtime` / `set_permissions` / `set_keepalive` / `.flush()` remains in production paths (the two leftovers — `diff_planner.rs::sync_mtimes` test helper and `win_fs.rs::clear_readonly_recursive` cleanup utility — are appropriately best-effort). **R42-F2 (Low)**: the new pipeline-streaming regression test only exercised `execute_sink_pipeline_streaming` directly, leaving `MultiStreamSender::queue`'s producer-side join-error-drain logic untested. Extracted the load-bearing match arms into a free `drain_pipeline_error(handle: JoinHandle<...>) -> eyre::Report` helper in `crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs`. `MultiStreamSender::queue` and `MultiStreamSender::finish` both now route their failure paths through it, so the wrapping reads consistently as "data plane pipeline failed: <cause>" or "data plane pipeline panicked: <join>" regardless of which branch noticed the failure first. Three new unit tests in a fresh `drain_pipeline_error_tests` mod: `drain_returns_pipeline_error_with_failed_prefix` (Ok(Err(_)) path), `drain_returns_panic_message_for_aborted_task` (Err(JoinError) panic path), `drain_diagnoses_clean_close_race` (Ok(Ok(_)) clean-close-race diagnostic — proves we don't silently swallow it). **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 396 pass / 0 fail (was 393, +3 drain-helper tests).
DEVLOG.md:150:**2026-05-05 16:00:00Z** - **FIX**: §2.7 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — closed `docs/plan/POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md` Round 1. **§1.1**: `let _ = file.flush().await` in `sink.rs::write_file_stream` now propagates as a `Status::internal`-style error via `with_context("flushing {dst}")?` — flush failure is a data-loss signal (user believes file is durable when it isn't). Six other "best-effort metadata" sites in `sink.rs` (block-complete mtime/perms, tar-shard mtime/perms, file-payload mtime, write_block_complete mtime/perms) plus three TCP socket tuning sites in `data_plane.rs` (`set_keepalive`, `set_send_buffer_size`, `set_recv_buffer_size`) plus two `local_worker.rs::single_file_mmap_copy` mtime sites converted from silent `let _ = ...` to `if let Err(e) = ... { log::warn!(...) }` so misconfigurations are visible without making them hard transfer errors. **§1.1b**: `MultiStreamSender::queue` in `crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs` no longer turns "tx.send failed" into the generic `"data plane pipeline closed unexpectedly"` string. `pipeline_handle` is now `Option<JoinHandle<...>>`; on send-failure, `take()` it, await, and surface the underlying error (`Ok(Err(e))` → `e.wrap_err("data plane pipeline failed")`, `Err(join)` → panic message). New regression test `pipeline_streaming_surfaces_underlying_sink_error` in `pipeline.rs::tests` uses a `FailingSink` to assert the underlying error message reaches the caller verbatim. **§1.2**: `TarShardExecutor` decision — kept with a thorough docstring explaining (a) gRPC-fallback streaming framing differs from TCP path's contiguous-buffer apply, requiring a `TarShardReceiver` trait extraction to unify, and (b) buffer-pool sizing benefits from real fallback throughput numbers. Deferred to post-0.1.0; tracked in `POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md` §1.2. **§1.3**: `WHITEPAPER.md` §3 "Data plane wire format" updated — `BLOCK_COMPLETE := 0x03 path_len:u32 path:bytes total_size:u64 mtime:i64 perms:u32` (was missing the trailing `mtime + perms` shipped in `a7d659f`); added an explanatory paragraph on why the metadata trails the terminator. `POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md` Round 1 status block flipped to "Closed 2026-05-05". **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 393 pass / 0 fail (was 392, +1 new pipeline-streaming regression test).
DEVLOG.md:152:**2026-05-05 15:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.5 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — `blit completions` now generates real shell-completion scripts via `clap_complete`, honoring the `README.md:33` "shell completions" promise. Reshaped `Completions(CompletionArgs)` into a subcommand group: `blit completions shell <SHELL>` (bash/zsh/fish/powershell/elvish) writes a clap_complete-generated script to stdout; `blit completions remote <REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` is the existing daemon-backed CompletePath RPC the generated shell scripts call internally for `server:/module/<Tab>` style remote-path completion. New dep `clap_complete = "4"` on `blit-cli`. Three lib unit tests added: `clap_definition_validates` (forces clap's `debug_assert`), `shell_completions_generate_for_bash` (script defines `_blit()` and references known verbs), `shell_completions_generate_for_each_supported_shell` (totality across `clap_complete::Shell`). New integration test `test_utils_completions_shell_bash_emits_script`. Existing tests `test_utils_completions`, `test_utils_completions_dirs_only`, `test_admin_completions_*` updated to call `blit completions remote <target>` (subcommand split). README.md `### Admin Utilities` has new install snippets for bash/zsh/fish; `find --pattern ".csv"` updated to `"*.csv"` to match the post-§2.4 glob semantics. CHANGELOG entry rewritten to describe the two completion subforms. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 392 pass / 0 fail (was 386, +3 cli tests + 2 list smart-dispatch from §2.3 + 1 shell-bash integration).
DEVLOG.md:156:**2026-05-04 22:00:00Z** - **FIX**: Round 41 review (R41-F1, R41-F2, R41-F3) of commits `0ca489b` + `090f5cd`. **R41-F1 (High)**: binary-rename sweep was incomplete — five test files (`diagnostics_dump.rs`, `remote_tcp_fallback.rs`, `remote_remote.rs`, `remote_checksum_negotiation.rs`, `remote_pull_mirror.rs`) still constructed `bin_dir.join("blit-cli[.exe]")`, so `cargo test --workspace` on a clean target tree failed with `spawn: No such file or directory`; the previous "385 pass" report only held because stale `target/release/blit-cli` was still around. All five updated to `"blit" / "blit.exe"`. Cosmetic panic-message strings in `remote_resume.rs`, `remote_regression.rs`, `remote_parity.rs`, `remote_push_single_file.rs` swept for consistency. **R41-F2 (High)**: bench scripts still pointed at the old binary — `scripts/bench_remote_remote.sh` (BLIT default + comment), `scripts/bench_10gbe.sh` (BLIT path), `scripts/bench_local_mirror.sh` (build invocation `--bin blit-cli` and resulting BLIT_BIN path) all updated. **R41-F3 (Medium)**: `find --pattern` glob set `literal_separator(false)`, letting `*` cross `/`. With the basename fallback this caused over-matches like `foo*.csv` matching `foo/bar.csv`. Switched to `literal_separator(true)` (POSIX shell-glob semantics; `*` does NOT cross `/`); basename fallback still gives "find files at any depth" via `*.csv`. Added regression test `test_admin_find_glob_star_does_not_cross_path_separator`. CLI `FindArgs.pattern` rustdoc rewritten to spell out the no-cross-separator behavior and the basename fallback. **Tests**: clean `target/`, `cargo test --workspace` 386 pass / 0 fail (was 385, +1 new R41-F3 test).
DEVLOG.md:158:**2026-05-04 21:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.1 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — renamed the produced CLI binary from `blit-cli` to `blit`, matching every doc/manpage/README example. `crates/blit-cli/Cargo.toml` keeps the workspace-member name `blit-cli` and adds `[[bin]] name = "blit"` so only the produced binary renames; cargo `--package blit-cli` and `-p blit-cli` references stay valid. Test helpers (`tests/common/mod.rs::TestContext::new`, `tests/single_file_copy.rs::cli_bin`) walk the build dir and previously hardcoded `"blit-cli"` / `"blit-cli.exe"`; updated to `"blit"` / `"blit.exe"`. CI artifact path in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and the binary list in `scripts/build-release.sh` + `scripts/windows/build-release.ps1` flipped accordingly. Per-platform run/bench scripts (`scripts/{linux,macos,windows}/run-journal-fastpath.{sh,ps1}`, `run-remote-fallback.sh`, `bench-local-mirror.ps1`) now reference `target/.../release/blit{.exe}`. Build-instruction strings in scripts updated from `--bin blit-cli` to `--bin blit`. Stale `target/release/blit-cli` and `target/debug/blit-cli` artifacts removed to confirm nothing was silently relying on them. **Tests**: `cargo build --release -p blit-cli --bin blit` produces `target/release/blit`; `cargo test --workspace` 385 pass / 0 fail.
DEVLOG.md:160:**2026-05-04 21:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.4 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — `find --pattern` now glob-matches via `globset`, replacing pre-0.1.0 substring containment. Daemon-side `stream_find_entries` in `service/admin.rs` builds a `globset::GlobBuilder` matcher once with `case_insensitive(!case_sensitive)` and `literal_separator(false)`; matcher tested against both the entry's relative path and its file-name basename so `--pattern '*.csv'` finds nested files via the basename fallback even without `**/`. Invalid globs surface as `Status::invalid_argument` from the daemon and propagate to a non-zero CLI exit. CLI rustdoc on `FindArgs.pattern` updated to describe glob syntax. CHANGELOG: removed "find uses substring matching" known-limitation row, added a glob behavior note under Admin Utilities. Two new integration tests in `crates/blit-cli/tests/admin_verbs.rs`: `test_admin_find_glob_invalid_pattern_rejected` (malformed `[unterminated` rejected) and `test_admin_find_glob_nested_with_double_star` (`**/*.csv` finds top + deep entries). Updated existing `test_admin_find_with_pattern` and `test_utils_find` / `test_utils_find_json` to use glob patterns (`*.csv`, `*.md`, `*.log`) instead of substring fragments. New daemon dep: `globset = "0.4.18"`. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 385 pass / 0 fail (was 383, +2 new glob tests).
DEVLOG.md:168:**2026-05-03 00:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Implemented Phase 2 of `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md`. Remote→remote `copy`/`mirror`/`move` now dispatch to destination-side `DelegatedPull` by default, with `--relay-via-cli` as the only legacy relay escape hatch. Added `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs` to build the same `TransferOperationSpec` as pull-sync, call `DelegatedPull`, surface stale-daemon/gate/connect/transfer errors without silent fallback, and print delegated summaries. Added env-gated test instrumentation in `blit-core::remote::instrumentation` so integration tests can assert the CLI sent zero data-plane payload bytes and never constructed `RemoteTransferSource` on the direct path, while `--relay-via-cli` does both. Reworked `crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_remote.rs` to cover direct byte-path isolation, gate-rejection no-fallback, stale destination `Unimplemented` no-fallback, and explicit relay. Updated CLI docs/help for `--relay-via-cli`. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo check -p blit-cli`; `cargo test -p blit-cli remote_remote_direct`; `cargo test -p blit-cli --test remote_remote`; `cargo test -p blit-core pull_sync_with_spec`; `cargo test -p blit-daemon delegated_pull`; `cargo test --workspace` (all passed; existing macOS FSEvents deprecation/F14 warnings remain).
DEVLOG.md:170:**2026-05-02 23:45:00Z** - **DOCS**: Round 18 finding R18-F1 fix — synced TODO.md with closed baseline + followup findings (commit `767f8ee`). Marked F2/F3/F4/F5/F7/F8/F9/F10/F11/F12/F13 + the pipeline-unification sequence as complete, leaving only F14 (FSEvents) and F15 (deferred logging). Round 19 follow-up R19-F1 + R19-F2 fix in commit-after-this: moved "Remote→remote re-evaluation" out of the executable Current Review Follow-up checklist into a new "Deferred design calls" subsection so it's not mistakenly picked up as the first unchecked actionable item; replaced the stale "16-round followup series" wording with a count-free phrase; backfilled this DEVLOG entry per R19-F2.
DEVLOG.md:172:**2026-05-02 23:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed F13 + F3 from `docs/reviews/codebase_review_2026-05-01.md` — the last remaining baseline polish items. **F13** (docs drift): with F2 making canonical-path containment always-on, the `use_chroot` config field was loaded-but-unused and the docs claimed enforcement that pre-F2 didn't exist. Removed `use_chroot` and `root_use_chroot` entirely from `ModuleConfig`/`RootExport`/`RootSpec`/`RawModule`/`RawDaemonSection`, the daemon startup banner, and `service::util::resolve_module`. TOML configs containing those keys are silently ignored (serde unknown-field tolerance). `docs/DAEMON_CONFIG.md`: removed the option from example, table, and added a new "Path containment" subsection explaining always-on enforcement with an honest TOCTOU caveat. Updated `docs/plan/WORKFLOW_PHASE_3.md`, `MASTER_WORKFLOW.md`, `BLIT_UTILS_PLAN.md`, `greenfield_plan_v6.md` to describe the post-F2 reality. Drive-by cleanup: deleted the orphaned `crates/blit-daemon/src/types.rs` and `crates/blit-daemon/src/config.rs` files (never declared as modules, dead code from a prior refactor). 5 test files migrated to drop `use_chroot` from their `ModuleSection` definitions. **F3** (reframed in triage as docs-only): added a "Trust Model and Network Exposure" section to `docs/DAEMON_CONFIG.md` stating explicitly that `0.0.0.0` default is intentional for a network file daemon, security comes from operator network controls + per-transfer tokens (not loopback-by-default), and TLS/mutual-auth fronting is out of scope. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (188 lib + 23 daemon + integration suites, all green). With F13 + F3 closed, the original 2026-05-01 baseline review has 13 of 15 findings closed; remaining are F14 (FSEvents deprecation) and F15 (structured logging — explicitly deferred).
DEVLOG.md:174:**2026-05-02 22:15:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 15 finding R15-F1 (Low) — F11's user-facing closure. The previous F11 fix added daemon ack-mismatch handling in `RemotePullClient::pull_sync`, but `transfers/endpoints.rs::ensure_remote_transfer_supported` rejected `--checksum` for ALL remote transfers before the new code could fire. F11's CLI behavior was therefore unreachable. Made the gate direction-aware: `ensure_remote_pull_supported` allows `--checksum` (the F11 ack negotiates), `ensure_remote_push_supported` keeps it rejected for push/remote-remote (no equivalent capability negotiation in the push protocol yet). New integration test `remote_checksum_negotiation.rs` spins up a daemon with `--no-server-checksums`, runs `blit copy --checksum server:/test/file.txt dest`, and asserts (a) the client refuses with a clear ack-mismatch error, (b) no file is copied via silent fallback. Companion test confirms the same command succeeds against a daemon with checksums enabled. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (188 lib + 23 daemon + 27 metrics-test bin + 2 new checksum-negotiation integration tests, all green).
DEVLOG.md:176:**2026-05-02 21:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed F5, F11, F12, F9 from `docs/reviews/codebase_review_2026-05-01.md` — the lower-priority items from the baseline triage. **F5** (Medium): replaced inc_active/dec_active scattered around `service/core.rs` with `TransferMetrics::enter_transfer() -> ActiveGuard`, an RAII handle that decrements on Drop so panics/cancellation can't leak the gauge. Moved `inc_purge` to dispatch boundary so it counts attempts (matching push/pull semantics) instead of successes. Module doc updated with the attempts/errors/gauge contract. 5 metrics tests including panic-recovery assertion. **F11** (Medium): pull_sync receive loop now stores `ack.server_checksums_enabled` on `RemotePullReport` and bails early at the ack if the user requested `--checksum` but the daemon has checksums disabled — silent fallback to size+mtime would lie about the comparison strength the user asked for. **F12** (Low–Medium): documented `blit check` equivalence model in CLI `--help` (new `CHECK_SEMANTICS_HELP` constant, `after_long_help` on `CheckArgs`) and `CheckArgs` rustdoc. Pinned the documented behavior with 6 unit tests covering: matching-files-zero-diffs, empty-dirs-not-in-equivalence, symlinks-skipped, file-vs-dir-mismatch-on-file-side, missing-on-dest, --one-way ignores extras. **F9** (Medium): split `execute_local_mirror` into a sync wrapper + new `execute_local_mirror_async`. The async core is callable from any Tokio runtime; the sync wrapper just builds a runtime and block_ons. 2 tests prove async-from-async-context works and the sync wrapper is unchanged. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (188 lib + 23 daemon + 27 metrics-test bin + integration suites, all green).
DEVLOG.md:186:**2026-05-02 16:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 9 finding R9-F1 (Low). The R8-F2 fix (rejecting stream EOF without UploadComplete) was previously verified by code review only — the new framing-helper unit tests covered R8-F1 size/cap rules but not the actual EOF path through `receive_fallback_data`. To make the function unit-testable without a real gRPC server, generic-ified its signature over `tokio_stream::Stream<Item = Result<ClientPushRequest, Status>>` (tonic::Streaming already implements this trait, so production callers are unchanged) and switched the loop body from `.message()` to `.next().await.transpose()` — semantically identical. 3 new regression tests in `data_plane::tests` drive the function over `tokio_stream::iter` of synthetic message vectors: EOF after `FileManifest`, EOF after `TarShardHeader`, and EOF on an empty stream (no UploadComplete ever). All three assert the rejection now lives in the test suite, not just code review. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (166 lib + 20 daemon + integration suites, all green).
DEVLOG.md:188:**2026-05-02 15:45:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 8 review findings R8-F1 and R8-F2 from `docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md`. Both bugs were in the daemon's gRPC push fallback receive loop (`receive_fallback_data` in `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs`) — the framing layer that runs *before* the shared `tar_safety` helper sees the buffer. **R8-F1** (Medium — DoS): the chunk accumulator was capped only by an 8 MiB initial reservation; the actual `Vec` grew unbounded. `archive_size == 0` skipped the overflow check entirely, and `archive_size > MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES` was bypassed at the framing layer. Reject zero archive_size when files are present, reject `archive_size > MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES` at TarShardHeader, and enforce both the declared size and the local cap on every chunk via a new `check_fallback_chunk_overflow` helper. **R8-F2** (Medium — silent partial success): stream EOF was treated as graceful end. But FileManifest and TarShardHeader remove entries from `pending` before bytes arrive, so a client could send a header, close the stream, and the final `pending.is_empty()` check would pass despite no data ever landing. Track `upload_complete_seen` and bail on EOF without UploadComplete or with `active.is_some()`. Extracted `validate_fallback_shard_archive_size` and `check_fallback_chunk_overflow` as private helpers so the rules are unit-testable without spinning up a full gRPC server. 7 new framing tests pin every boundary condition (zero size, at-cap, above cap, declared overflow, local-cap overflow, u64 overflow, within-bounds). **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (166 lib + 17 daemon + integration suites, all green).
DEVLOG.md:190:**2026-05-02 14:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Round 7 cleanup — extracted shared `tar_safety` primitive in `blit-core/src/remote/transfer/tar_safety.rs` and migrated all three tar-shard receive sites onto it: `pull.rs::apply_pull_tar_shard` (gRPC fallback), `sink.rs::write_tar_shard_payload` (TCP data plane + local-local), `blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs::apply_tar_shard_sync` (daemon push receive). The migration closed a previously-latent High-severity bug on the daemon side: `apply_tar_shard_sync` was calling `Entry::unpack`, so an authenticated push client could ship a tar shard with a Symlink entry at a benign path pointing at `/etc/passwd` and a later push to that path would write through the symlink — same R5-F2 class as the pull side, but server-side. New regression test `apply_tar_shard_rejects_symlink_entry` proves the daemon now rejects non-regular entries up front. The shared helper also brought R6-F1 size bounds and R6-F3 mtime/permissions preservation to `sink.rs` and the daemon, where they were missing. 8 new unit tests in `tar_safety::tests`; net +8 lib tests workspace-wide (166 total). **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` all green.
DEVLOG.md:194:**2026-05-02 11:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 5 review findings R5-F1 through R5-F5 from `docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md`. **R5-F1** (High — data-loss vector): `delete_listed_paths` used a lexical `target.starts_with(dest_root)` check that lets `dest_root.join("../victim")` pass. Routed every wire-supplied path through `path_safety::safe_join`, switched `RemotePullReport::paths_to_delete` from `Vec<PathBuf>` to `Vec<String>` so wire input never gets blessed into a path before validation, and added 4 unit tests in `delete_list_safety_tests` covering parent-traversal, absolute paths, root-self-reference, and the happy path. **R5-F2** (High — wire-controlled symlink/hardlink creation): `apply_pull_tar_shard` was calling `Entry::unpack` which honors tar's symlink/hardlink/device entry types. Rewrote to reject anything that isn't `EntryType::Regular` or `EntryType::Continuous`, validate the path through `validate_wire_path`, copy bytes manually, and verify per-entry size matches the corresponding `FileHeader.size`. Added 4 unit tests including a hand-crafted malicious tar with `../escape.txt` in its header. **R5-F3** (Medium — DoS via huge `archive_size`): `Vec::with_capacity(header.archive_size as usize)` was a daemon-controlled allocation. Added `MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES = 256 MiB` cap, modest initial reservation (capped at 1 MiB), per-chunk overflow check against `declared_size` and the local cap, and a final length check on `TarShardComplete`. **R5-F4** (Low — comment lied about validation): `filter_from_spec` claimed to validate glob patterns but only copied them into `FileFilter` for later silent dropping. Now compiles each include/exclude pattern through `globset::Glob::new` at the spec normalizer; 2 new tests pin rejection of malformed brackets. **R5-F5** (Low — DEVLOG ordering): added this header and moved newest entries to the top so review workflow doesn't depend on knowing how to find appended-at-the-bottom changes. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (152+ lib tests, all integration suites green).
DEVLOG.md:196:**2026-05-02 09:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Step 4C — tar-shard support on the gRPC pull fallback. The push gRPC fallback runs the unified planner and emits TarShardHeader/Chunk/Complete for batched small-file workloads; the pull side previously sent files one-by-one via a hand-written loop. New `GrpcServerStreamingSink` (mirror of `GrpcFallbackSink`) in `blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs` lets the daemon's `stream_via_grpc` run `execute_sink_pipeline` with the same planner output as the TCP data plane. Proto adds three new ServerPullMessage variants (fields 13/14/15) reusing the existing TarShard message types. Client receive loop in `pull_sync` adds three match arms with an `InProgressShard` buffer and an `apply_pull_tar_shard` extractor (mirror of the daemon's push-side `apply_tar_shard_sync`). New integration test `test_pull_grpc_fallback_many_small_files` exercises 50 small files via `--force-grpc`. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (all green); `cargo test -p blit-cli --test remote_parity` (5 tests including the new one, all pass).
DEVLOG.md:200:**2026-05-02 06:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed R2-F1, R4-F1, R4-F2, R4-F4 from `docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md`. (1) DiffPlanner's `local_needs_copy` now has concrete behavior for every `ComparisonMode` (SizeOnly, IgnoreTimes, Force, Checksum, SizeMtime/Unspecified) instead of silently falling through to size+mtime; 10 new per-mode tests. (2) Daemon PullSync handler routes through `NormalizedTransferOperation::from_spec` so spec_version mismatch and unknown enum values become hard `invalid_argument` errors at the wire boundary. (3) `ignore_existing` is now an orthogonal `bool` field on `TransferOperationSpec` (proto field 10; old enum value 5 reserved); `compare_mode = Force && ignore_existing` is rejected at the spec normalizer; CLI adds `conflicts_with = "force"` on `--ignore-existing`; orchestrator threads the flag through `LocalMirrorOptions → LocalDiffInputs` so local-local copies enforce it through the same planner as remote paths. (4) Refreshed stale proto comments referring to removed `PullSyncHeader` and the old "supports_filter_spec fallback" plan. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (all green, 144+ core tests).
DEVLOG.md:202:**2026-05-02 04:15:00Z** - **STEP 4A (PullSync wire migration to TransferOperationSpec)**: With no backwards-compat constraint into the next release, replaced the bool-soup `PullSyncHeader` (5 booleans for comparison mode + separate fields for resume/mirror/force_grpc) with the unified `TransferOperationSpec` defined in step 2. `ClientPullMessage::Header` is now `ClientPullMessage::Spec`. `PullSyncHeader` deleted from the proto entirely. Daemon parses the spec and translates its `ComparisonMode` enum onto the existing internal `CompareMode` via a new `compare_mode_to_internal` helper that also splits out `IgnoreExisting` as the orthogonal axis it actually is. Client (`RemotePullClient::pull_sync`) constructs the spec from CLI args, advertising full `PeerCapabilities` (resume, tar shards, data plane TCP, filter spec — all true), and defaulting `mirror_mode` to `MirrorMode::FilteredSubset` rather than the old "any-mirror-mode-true" boolean. Filter not yet honored on the daemon — that's step 4B. **The bool-soup → typed-enum win is real**: nonsensical combinations like `size_only=true && checksum=true && ignore_times=true` are no longer constructible. **Tests**: 214 pass (unchanged) — pure wire-shape migration, behavior preserved end-to-end.
DEVLOG.md:206:**2026-05-02 03:00:00Z** - **F1 follow-up (R1 fixes)**: Acted on `docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md` round 1. **R1-F1** (Medium, real bug): `validate_wire_path` accepted single-leading-backslash forms like `\Windows\System32` on Unix because `Path::components` treats backslash as a normal character there. Extended `looks_like_windows_absolute` to flag any input starting with `\`. Tests added for `\foo`, `\foo\bar`, bare `\`. **R1-F3** (Low): wire path `"."` previously normalized to empty, getting the same single-file treatment as the legitimate empty-path case. `validate_wire_path` now rejects non-empty inputs that produce zero normal components (`"."`, `"./"`, `"./."`) — those are directory hints, not file paths. Fallout: the daemon's `resolve_relative_path` (used for list/find/du request paths where `.` legitimately means "module root") needed to disambiguate between request-path and file-path contexts. Added a request-path-context fold that maps `""`, `"."`, `"./"` etc. to `PathBuf::from(".")` before invoking strict validation; file-path callers (`resolve_manifest_relative_path`, sink writes) keep using the strict validator directly. **R1-F2** (Medium): `safe_join` is lexical only — `dest_root/symlink/file` lexically passes even when `symlink` escapes the root. Reviewer correctly identified this as F2 (chroot/canonical-containment) territory, not an F1 fix. Documented the lexical-only contract prominently in `path_safety.rs` module doc with an explicit pointer to F2 for canonical-parent containment. Updated `TODO.md` F2 entry to call out the symlink-traversal concern. **Tests**: 214 pass (was 213) — net +1 from R1-F1 leading-backslash test minus zero from R1-F3 (the existing `just_dot_normalizes_to_empty` test was rewritten as `just_dot_rejected` covering three inputs in one test).
DEVLOG.md:208:**2026-05-02 02:30:00Z** - **STEP 3a (DiffPlanner extraction, orchestrator migration)**: New `blit-core::remote::transfer::diff_planner` module consolidates the diff + payload-planning stage. Public API: `plan_local_mirror(headers, LocalDiffInputs) -> PlannedPayloads` is the single entry point the orchestrator's local-mirror path now calls; under the hood it composes `filter_unchanged` (the comparison-filter stage previously named `filter_headers_for_copy` and inlined in `orchestrator.rs`) with the existing `plan_transfer_payloads`. Translates the proto `ComparisonMode` enum onto the `Option<ChecksumType>` that the existing comparison primitives consume — `Checksum` enables Blake3, all other variants (`Unspecified`, `SizeMtime`, `SizeOnly`, `IgnoreTimes`, `IgnoreExisting`, `Force`) currently fall back to size+mtime. The non-`SizeMtime` variants are accepted at the API surface so callers can pass them through; their proper behavior arrives with step 4 (pull_sync.rs migration brings the comparison strategies that today live as `PullSyncHeader` bools). Orchestrator's local-mirror path migrated: the previous inline scan → spawn_blocking(filter_headers_for_copy) → plan_transfer_payloads sequence is now scan → spawn_blocking(plan_local_mirror) — same effect, single chokepoint, smaller call site. Dead `filter_headers_for_copy` removed. `ChecksumType` gained `PartialEq + Eq` so the diff_planner unit tests can assert mappings. **Push and pull origin paths NOT yet migrated** — those land in step 3b and step 4 respectively. **Tests**: 213 pass (+4 new diff_planner unit tests covering the ComparisonMode→ChecksumType mapping for every variant, and the filter_unchanged identity-vs-modified behavior), 0 failures. Behavior preservation verified by the unchanged orchestrator integration tests (admin verbs, remote_remote, remote_pull_mirror, remote_resume, remote_tcp_fallback all still pass).
DEVLOG.md:212:**2026-05-02 01:15:00Z** - **F1 (path safety)**: Centralized receive-side path validation in `blit-core/src/path_safety.rs`. Two helpers: `validate_wire_path(s) -> PathBuf` (pure validation, normalized form) and `safe_join(root, s) -> PathBuf` (validate + join, with empty input returning root unchanged for the single-file destination case). Rejects: `..` components (not just substrings — the previous tar-shard `rel.contains("..")` check was too weak and also rejected legitimate filenames containing literal `..`), absolute paths, Windows drive prefixes (`C:\…`), UNC and DOS device paths (`\\?\…`, `\\server\share\…`), root components, embedded NUL bytes. Migrated `pull.rs::sanitize_relative_path` and daemon `service/util.rs::resolve_relative_path` / `resolve_manifest_relative_path` to thin wrappers that delegate to the shared module — single chokepoint, no drift between sanitizers. Applied at all five receive-side sites the review identified: `sink.rs:218` (streamed file write), `sink.rs:404` + `:426` (tar shard entry validation + per-entry join), `sink.rs:462` (resume block write), `sink.rs:498` (block completion), and the gRPC fallback block-hash path in `pull.rs:532`. Wire-boundary defense in depth: `pipeline.rs::read_file_header` and per-entry path read in `read_tar_shard` validate at the moment paths arrive off the socket, so unsafe headers never enter the FileHeader stream. **Tests**: 209 passed (was 185 baseline) — 16 new `path_safety::tests` covering the validator surface (`..`, absolute Unix, Windows drive, UNC, NUL, valid `..`-containing filenames, deep paths, Unicode, trailing slash, just-dot), 8 new sink-level integration tests confirming `FsTransferSink::write_file_stream` rejects malicious wire paths before any filesystem write and that the legitimate single-file empty-path case still works. **Verification**: `cargo check --workspace` clean; `cargo test --workspace` 0 failures.
DEVLOG.md:220:**2026-04-24 02:54:00Z** - **ACTION**: Completed the remaining UX feedback items #4, #5, #6, #7, #8, #9, #10 from `docs/ux-feedback-migrate-games-poc.md`. #4 TTY auto-progress: `TransferArgs::effective_progress()` enables the progress indicator when stdout is a TTY and `--json` is off, matching rsync/rclone; piped stdout stays silent for scripts. #8 `--help` rsync docs: `after_long_help` on `TransferArgs` appends a PATHS section summarizing the trailing-slash rules to `blit copy/mirror/move --help`. #6/#7/#5 banner cleanup: demoted the `starting copy` line to stderr and dropped the version prefix; added `collapse_slashes()` display-only normalization so script-appended `//` no longer shows in the banner (semantics unchanged); under `-v`, print a follow-up line when rsync resolution materially rewrote the destination. #9 flag grouping: split `TransferArgs` flags across clap `help_heading` buckets (Options / Comparison / Reliability / Performance/debug) so first-time users see common flags before niche debug knobs. #10 `blit diagnostics dump SRC DST [--json]`: new subcommand emits a pasteable snapshot (version, invocation argv, parsed endpoints, rsync resolution, local filesystem caps, free/total disk, same-device check) without performing a transfer — built for one-shot bug reports. Added `sysinfo = "0.31"` (disk-only) to blit-cli, exposed `source_is_contents`/`dest_is_container` as `pub(crate)`, wrote 3 smoke tests pinning the JSON shape. Updated `blit(1)` manpage SYNOPSIS and DIAGNOSTICS sections. **Tests**: 170 passed (added 3), clippy clean.
DEVLOG.md:222:**2026-04-24 02:32:00Z** - **ACTION**: Addressed UX feedback items #1 and #3 from `docs/ux-feedback-migrate-games-poc.md`. Added `TransferOutcome` enum (`Transferred` / `JournalSkip` / `UpToDate` / `SourceEmpty`) to `LocalMirrorSummary` so the CLI can emit distinct, honest messages for the three legitimate zero-files cases instead of the ambiguous `Copy complete: 0 files, 0 B`. `FastPathDecision::NoWork` now carries an `examined` count to split empty-source from already-up-to-date; the single-file path also sets `UpToDate` when `skip_unchanged` short-circuits. CLI `print_summary` in `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/local.rs` matches on outcome and suppresses the `• Throughput / Workers used` line unless verbose, ≥1 MiB, or `>1` file copied. JSON output now includes `outcome` and `files_examined`. Strengthened `single_file_copy_idempotent` to pin `Copy complete: 1 files` on first run and `Up to date` on second run — would have caught the regression where the single-file rerun emitted misleading `Copy complete: 0 files`. **Tests**: 167 passed, clippy clean.
DEVLOG.md:224:**2026-04-14 20:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Completed unified-pipeline remediation across all transfer paths. Previously only local→local and gRPC-fallback push routed through `execute_sink_pipeline`; multi-stream TCP push and both daemon pull paths had bespoke dispatch code. Added `execute_sink_pipeline_streaming(source, sinks, payload_rx, prefetch, progress)` in `crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs` and made the one-shot form a thin wrapper. Rewrote `MultiStreamSender` in `crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs` as a lightweight `payload_tx` + `JoinHandle` shell — deleted `data_plane_sink_worker`, batch-splitting helpers, and `StreamStats` (~130 lines). Converted both daemon pull paths in `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs` to use the pipeline; deleted `handle_pull_stream` and `handle_pull_stream_streaming` (~125 lines). Deleted dead modules: `transfer_engine.rs` (WorkerFactory trait + task-channel infra), `transfer_facade/` (TaskAggregator, PlannerEvent, build_local_plan, stream_local_plan), and `RetryableTask` from `transfer_plan.rs` — 1139 lines total. Updated `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` with the unified-pipeline diagram and source/sink matrix. Added `blit(1)` command-summary hints and expanded `TransferArgs` doc-comments for `--help`. **Tests**: 152 passed (87 core + 65 integration), clippy clean. **Remaining**: end-to-end 10 GbE benchmark runs.
DEVLOG.md:226:**2026-04-07 18:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Completed all Phase 4 production hardening items that don't require hardware. Created `CHANGELOG.md` with full 0.1.0 feature inventory. Added `docs/plan/AI_TELEMETRY_ANALYSIS.md` scoping statistical anomaly detection, tuning recommendations, and diagnostics (all local, no ML, opt-in). Created `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with cargo fmt/clippy, tri-platform tests, and release artifact builds. Added `scripts/build-release.sh` and `scripts/windows/build-release.ps1`. Updated TODO to mark packaging, CI, telemetry, logging review, install docs, and release notes complete. **Remaining**: benchmark runs (need hardware), RDMA (post-release), ReFS privilege investigation (Windows).
DEVLOG.md:228:**2026-04-07 17:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Rewrote `docs/DAEMON_CONFIG.md` fixing multiple inaccuracies: wrong port (50051→9031), wrong TOML format (`[modules.backup]`→`[[module]]`), wrong URL syntax. Added client config directory paths, mDNS section, and expanded service installation templates for all platforms. Performed logging/error audit: removed duplicate println/eprintln debug output from block clone paths (`copy/windows.rs`, `copy/file_copy/mod.rs`). Added expect() messages to bare unwrap() calls in `remote/endpoint.rs` and `remote/push/client/mod.rs`. No dbg!()/todo!() found in production code. Full structured logging migration deferred to post-release. **Tests**: `cargo build` (clean).
DEVLOG.md:232:**2026-04-07 15:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Updated README, ARCHITECTURE, and documentation. Added macOS to supported platforms. Expanded blit-utils feature bullet and usage examples. Replaced `cargo run` examples with direct binary names. Expanded ARCHITECTURE.md with blit-utils file structure, filesystem capability probing section, copy operations primary/fallback table, and updated test categories. Created `docs/cli/blit-utils.1.md` man page with full synopsis, options, examples, and exit codes. Cross-referenced blit-utils(1) from blit(1) and blit-daemon(1) SEE ALSO. Updated BLIT_UTILS_PLAN.md implementation checklist. Fixed `edition = "2024"` typo to `"2021"` in `crates/blit-utils/Cargo.toml`. **Tests**: `cargo build` (clean).
DEVLOG.md:244:**2025-01-29 12:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Added resume integration tests (`remote_resume.rs`) covering partial file resume, identical file optimization, and gRPC fallback path. Security hardening: MAX_BLOCK_SIZE (64 MiB) enforced on both client and server to prevent OOM attacks from malicious block_size values. Export added to `copy/mod.rs`. Updated TODO.md to mark P0 parity tests and refactoring items as complete. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` (64 passed).
DEVLOG.md:246:**2025-01-29 10:00:00Z** - **FIX**: Addressed Gemini code review findings for remote block-level resume. (1) Fixed critical memory vulnerability: `compute_block_hashes` in `pull.rs` and both `stream_via_data_plane_resume`/`stream_via_block_resume_grpc` in `pull_sync.rs` now stream files in block-sized chunks instead of loading entire files into memory. (2) Fixed stop-and-wait protocol in data plane path: `stream_via_data_plane_resume` now pipelines all block hash requests upfront (Phase 1), collects all responses (Phase 2), then processes files (Phase 3) - eliminates per-file RTT penalty. gRPC fallback keeps simpler stop-and-wait since it's for diagnostic use only. **Tests**: `cargo build --package blit-daemon --package blit-cli` (passed).
DEVLOG.md:250:**2025-01-28 15:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Implemented remote block-level resume (gRPC fallback path). Extended protocol with `BlockHashRequest`, `BlockHashList`, `BlockTransfer`, and `BlockTransferComplete` messages. Server-side: `stream_via_block_resume_grpc` in `pull_sync.rs` identifies Modified files (resume-eligible), requests block hashes from client, compares Blake3 hashes, sends only differing blocks via gRPC. Client-side: `compute_block_hashes` helper, handlers for all block messages with seek+write for in-place block updates. **Tests**: `cargo test -p blit-core` (63 passed).
DEVLOG.md:266:**2025-01-26 13:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Removed all backwards-compatibility code from `DataPlaneSession` per "no tech debt" directive (project hasn't shipped yet). Eliminated `SessionBuffer` enum, legacy `from_stream()` signature, `connect_with_pool` wrapper, and single-buffer fallback path. Now `DataPlaneSession::from_stream` and `::connect` both require `Arc<BufferPool>` and always use double-buffered I/O. Updated callers: `MultiStreamSender::connect` passes pool directly (removed `Some()` wrapper), `pull.rs::handle_pull_stream` creates a pool sized for single-stream operation. Clean compile, all buffer pool tests pass. **Tests**: `cargo check --workspace`; `cargo test -p blit-core buffer`.
DEVLOG.md:270:**2025-01-26 12:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Implemented async read-ahead pipeline with double-buffering in `DataPlaneSession::send_file`. When a buffer pool is available, acquires two buffers and uses `tokio::join!` to overlap disk reads with network writes. While buffer A is being written to network, buffer B is filled from disk, then roles are swapped. This hides disk latency behind network latency for improved throughput. Falls back to single-buffer path when no pool is available. **Tests**: `cargo test -p blit-core`.
DEVLOG.md:274:**2025-01-26 10:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Implemented buffer pool for reusable allocations (25GbE performance work). Added `BufferPool` struct to `blit-core/src/buffer.rs` with rclone-inspired design: semaphore-based memory budget control, RAII `PoolBuffer` guards for automatic return, statistics tracking. Critically, the pool does NOT define hardcoded defaults—it accepts parameters from the orchestrator/auto_tune system so buffer sizes remain dynamically tuned based on runtime conditions. **Tests**: `cargo test -p blit-core pool_tests`.
DEVLOG.md:276:**2025-01-21 13:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Verified and documented remote↔remote transfer support. Implementation was already complete via `RemoteTransferSource` abstraction in `blit-core/src/remote/transfer/source.rs`. Removed debug print statement from `RemoteTransferSource::open_file`. Existing integration test `remote_remote.rs` covers dual-daemon server-to-server copy. Updated all integration tests (`remote_parity.rs`, `remote_pull_mirror.rs`, `remote_tcp_fallback.rs`, `remote_move.rs`, `remote_remote.rs`) to include `--yes` flag for mirror/move commands following the destructive operation prompts implementation. **Tests**: All remote integration tests now use `--yes` to skip confirmation prompts.
DEVLOG.md:282:**2025-11-19 16:55:00Z** - **ACTION**: Added integration tests for remote transfer parity. `crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_parity.rs` verifies that both push and pull operations correctly negotiate the TCP data plane (checking for absence of fallback tag) and respect `--force-grpc` (checking for fallback tag). Fixed a bug in `RemotePullClient` where leading `./` components in file paths caused the mirror purge logic to delete valid files. **Tests**: `cargo test -p blit-cli --test remote_parity`; `cargo test -p blit-cli --test remote_tcp_fallback`.
DEVLOG.md:284:**2025-11-15 01:25:00Z** - **FIX**: Hardened the push client so it only streams files the daemon explicitly requested. We now track the requested set coming from `FilesToUpload`, prune unrequested files out of payload batches, and log/skipping anything the daemon didn’t ask for. This prevents the upload queue from desyncing (which caused the recent “unexpected file entry … connection closed” failures) while we continue investigating the underlying need-list mismatch. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo build -p blit-core`.
DEVLOG.md:294:**2025-11-10 12:20:00Z** - **ACTION**: Shortened manifest→payload latency and hardened permission handling for remote pushes. The daemon now flushes `FilesToUpload` batches immediately during enumeration, so first payloads launch within milliseconds even on 300 k+ file manifests. On the client, unreadable files are logged in red and filtered out before any payload planning, keeping the negotiated TCP path active instead of falling back to slow gRPC streams. Remaining gap: data-plane concurrency still single-threaded, so next step is implementing multiplexed streams driven by auto-tuned worker counts to hit 10 GbE. **Tests**: `cargo build -p blit-core`; `cargo build -p blit-daemon`.
DEVLOG.md:296:**2025-11-10 09:05:00Z** - **PROGRESS**: Wired the remote auto-tuning path through both data-plane and fallback streaming. `RemotePushClient` now feeds the tuned chunk size into every transfer mode and passes the tuned stream counts to the TCP sender + control-plane payload builder so tar-shard construction scales with observed bandwidth. The daemon pull data-plane adopts the same parameters, ensuring both push and pull honor the shared heuristics. Updated TODO/plan docs accordingly. **Tests**: `cargo build -p blit-core`; `cargo build -p blit-daemon`.
DEVLOG.md:304:**2025-11-07 21:45:00Z** - **NOTE**: Remote pull parity gap. Field run (`./blit-cli mirror -p -v 10.1.10.180://Steam/ /mnt/...`) showed no progress output and sustained ~28 Mbps because pulls are still pure gRPC streams (64 KiB chunks) with no hybrid transport. Running the reverse direction surfaces output but tops out around 400 Mbps. Added a P0 plan/TODO item to mandate absolute throughput/feature parity across all src/dst combinations; remote pull must reuse the streaming/data-plane stack and emit `-p`/`-v` progress before GA.  
DEVLOG.md:306:**2025-11-07 18:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Hardened remote push memory usage and scheduling. `drain_pending_headers` now removes dispatched headers (so the manifest lookup stays bounded), the push loop no longer uses a biased `tokio::select!` and drains the manifest channel after every daemon response, and `RemotePushReport` only tracks the requested-file count to avoid cloning every path. Added a regression + ignored stress test (`drain_pending_headers_handles_one_million_entries`) so we can capture RSS/timing data for 1 M-entry manifests on demand, and documented that operators are expected to secure the data plane via trusted networks/SSH tunnels instead of in-proc TLS. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo check`; `cargo test -p blit-core drain_pending_headers_removes_matched_entries`.
DEVLOG.md:316:**2025-10-28 22:05:00Z** - **ACTION**: Parallelised daemon tar-shard handling so streaming mirrors no longer block while unpacking. Introduced `TarShardExecutor` (bounded `JoinSet` with semaphore) that decodes shards on `spawn_blocking`, accumulates stats as workers finish, and reuses the same path for gRPC fallback. Throughput validation on `skippy`/`mycroft` still pending once new binaries are deployed. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo check -p blit-daemon`.
DEVLOG.md:320:**2025-10-28 23:55:00Z** - **ACTION**: Began throughput push tuning. CLI now pipelines tar-shard preparation (data plane shares the same buffered stream as control-plane fallback) and the daemon batches need-list flushes instead of emitting single-file requests. Added verbose `[data-plane] …` logging around the daemon’s TCP handler to catch the “Connection reset by peer” root cause (currently seeing `upload_tx send failed` when the channel closes). **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo check -p blit-core`; `cargo check -p blit-daemon`.
DEVLOG.md:322:**2025-10-28 00:10:00Z** - **ACTION**: Modularised the change journal. Split the single file into `change_journal/{mod,types,snapshot,tracker,util}.rs`, isolating platform capture logic per OS and keeping `ChangeTracker` implementation focused on persistence + probing. Orchestrator now consumes the same re-exported API. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo check -p blit-core`.
DEVLOG.md:328:**2025-10-26 21:56:50Z** - **ACTION**: Reworked remote push streaming to honour the 1 s heartbeat rule. The CLI prints an immediate startup banner, logs manifest progress every second, and the daemon now flushes need-list batches as soon as they’re discovered—starting the TCP listener (or gRPC fallback) on the very first batch so transfers begin before manifest enumeration completes. **Tests**: `cargo test -p blit-core`; `cargo test -p blit-daemon`; `cargo test -p blit-cli --test remote_tcp_fallback`.
DEVLOG.md:332:**2025-10-26 23:40:00Z** - **ACTION**: Implemented streaming manifest negotiation for remote pushes. CLI now enumerates files via a bounded channel (64 entries) feeding gRPC stream, while the daemon batches need-list responses on the fly and applies back-pressure. Control-plane fallback streams file data in 1 MiB chunks, eliminating whole-file buffering. Updated TODO/workflow docs to mark the blocker resolved and added a follow-up stress-test task. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo check`; `cargo test`.
DEVLOG.md:334:**2025-10-26 02:33:00Z** - **ACTION**: Coordinated with WingPT on ReFS clone validation. Added info-level logging when `FSCTL_DUPLICATE_EXTENTS_TO_FILE` succeeds and captured cloned vs fallback runs (`logs/windows/bench_local_windows_4gb_clone_{20251026T015438Z,20251026T020337Z}.log`). ETW trace `logs/windows/refs_clone_profile_20251026T022401Z.etl` shows the clone IOCTL completes in ~15 ms while metadata/ACL updates dominate remaining runtime (~0.28 s vs robocopy 0.17 s). Benchmark harnesses now honour `BLIT_BENCH_ROOT` on all platforms. Added TODO to prototype a metadata-lite clone mode for further tuning. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo check`; `cargo test -p blit-core`.
DEVLOG.md:336:**2025-10-28 01:15:00Z** - **ACTION**: Reworked remote push streaming so need-list batches flush immediately. `RemotePushClient` now biases response handling, streams gRPC fallback payloads as soon as headers arrive, and only emits `UploadComplete` when queues drain, guaranteeing data movement within one second even when the daemon negotiates fallback. Added helper to finish lingering data-plane sessions after the control loop exits. **Tests**: `cargo fmt -p blit-core`; `cargo check -p blit-core`; `timeout 120s cargo test -p blit-cli remote_tcp_fallback -- --nocapture`.
DEVLOG.md:338:**2025-10-26 20:23:35Z** - **ACTION**: Manifest/need-list streaming flagged as blocker. Paused development until remote push can negotiate arbitrarily large payloads without exhausting memory. Added TODO (`manifest_streaming_plan`) and updated workflows to prioritise CLI/daemon streaming refactor plus back-pressure handling.
DEVLOG.md:340:**2025-10-25 03:15:00Z** - **ACTION**: Added Linux metadata snapshot backend for the change journal. `LinuxSnapshot` records device/inode/ctime (+mtime) and enables the planner to skip no-op runs on ext-based filesystems. Added `scripts/linux/run-journal-fastpath.sh` and captured validation run (`logs/linux/journal-fastpath-20251025T031500Z.log`, zero-change mirror in ~3 ms). **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo check -p blit-core --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`; `bash scripts/linux/run-journal-fastpath.sh`.
DEVLOG.md:342:**2025-10-25 02:20:00Z** - **ACTION**: Implemented macOS FSEvents snapshot backend for the change journal. `MacSnapshot` now records device FSID, current FSEvents ID, and root mtime; orchestrator refreshes checkpoints so zero-change runs can skip planning on macOS once verified. Added `scripts/macos/run-journal-fastpath.sh` for validation and updated TODO/workflows. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo check -p blit-core --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`.
DEVLOG.md:346:**2025-10-24 03:05:00Z** - **ACTION**: Added filesystem journal fast-path for local mirrors on Windows (USN checkpoints) with persistent cache + CLI integration. The orchestrator now short-circuits planning when the USN journal reports no changes, updating the cache after each run; macOS FSEvents and Linux fanotify remain TODO. Introduced `change_journal.rs` with serde-backed storage and unit tests. **Tests**: `cargo check -p blit-core`; `cargo test -p blit-core change_journal`.
DEVLOG.md:352:**2025-10-23 23:05:00Z** - **ACTION**: Remote mirror pushes now purge stale remote files by reusing the daemon's Purge helper; `PushSummary` reports `entries_deleted` and the CLI surfaces the deletion count. Added server-side purge helpers, mirrored fallback handling, and coverage for extraneous deletions. **Tests**: `cargo build -p blit-core`; `cargo check`; `cargo test -p blit-daemon`; `cargo test -p blit-cli`; `cargo test -p blit-utils`.
DEVLOG.md:356:**2025-10-21 01:45:00Z** - **ACTION**: Reworked small-file heuristics. `TransferFacade` now samples workloads and flushes tar shards once batches hit ~8 MiB or ≈1 k entries (auto-detecting ≤64 KiB averages), with leftover bundles forced through tar in small-file mode. `transfer_plan::build_plan` mirrors the new thresholds, and regression tests cover both the shard path and the raw-bundle fallback. `cargo test -p blit-core transfer_facade` passes.
DEVLOG.md:358:**2025-10-20 22:55:00Z** - **ACTION**: Implemented daemon config loader + CLI overrides. `blit-daemon` now reads `/etc/blit/config.toml` (or `--config`), merges module definitions and default root, respects `--bind`, `--port`, `--root`, `--no-mdns`, `--mdns-name`, and warns when falling back to the working directory. Tests updated to use the new constructor. `cargo test --all-targets` remains green.
DEVLOG.md:362:**2025-10-21 03:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Added planner task instrumentation. `TransferFacade` now tracks tar/raw/large task counts and bytes, exposing them via `LocalPlanFinal` → `LocalMirrorSummary`. Performance history records these stats and the orchestrator seeds small/medium shard thresholds from the most recent runs before planning. Updated PowerShell harness earlier still applies; `cargo test -p blit-core` remains clean.
DEVLOG.md:365:**2025-10-23 04:15:10Z** - **ACTION**: CLI remote surface aligned with plan v6. `blit copy|mirror|move` now accept `host:/module/path` endpoints (module sub-paths supported), reusing the existing push/pull transports; redundant push/pull mentions removed from docs. Daemon push handling honours per-transfer destination paths, and `blit list` now interacts with the daemon (`ListModules` / `List`) or falls back to local listings. `cargo clippy` and `cargo test -p blit-core` run clean.
DEVLOG.md:367:**2025-10-25 20:01:22Z** - **ACTION**: Added integration coverage for the hybrid transport fallback. New test (`crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_tcp_fallback.rs`) spins up `blit-daemon --force-grpc-data`, runs `blit mirror --force-grpc` against `server:/module/`, and asserts the run completes with gRPC fallback messaging and remote files present. Helper scripts now use `--config-dir` exclusively. **Tests**: `cargo build --bin blit-daemon --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`; `cargo test -p blit-cli --test remote_tcp_fallback --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`.
DEVLOG.md:369:**2025-10-25 22:54:02Z** - **RESULT**: Verified remote fallback on skippy/mycroft. Normal runs negotiated the TCP data plane; forcing `--force-grpc` logged `[gRPC fallback]` and mirrored successfully (`logs/remote/skippy.internal_force_20251025T225340Z.log`, `logs/remote/mycroft.internal_force_20251025T225340Z.log`).
DEVLOG.md:370:**2025-10-26 00:09:53Z** - **RESULT**: Captured refreshed large-file benchmarks across all platforms to close out Phase 2.5 evidence. Linux 1 GiB run (`logs/linux/bench_local_linux_20251025T230101Z.log`) shows blit 1.427 s vs rsync 3.206 s (~225 %). macOS 1 GiB/4 GiB runs (`logs/macos/bench_local_20251025T231137Z/bench.log`, `logs/macos/bench_local_20251025T235415Z/bench.log`) landed at 0.712 s vs 2.427 s and 2.823 s vs 9.721 s respectively. Windows NTFS 1 GiB (`logs/windows/bench_local_windows_20251025T233442Z.log`) is within ~7 % of robocopy (1.619 s vs 1.516 s), while the ReFS 4 GiB case (`logs/windows/bench_local_windows_4gb_20251025T235715Z.log`) still trails (0.374 s vs 0.155 s, ~41 %). Added a TrueNAS/ZFS datapoint (`logs/truenas/bench_local_zfs_20251026T004021Z.log`): blit averaged 0.879 s vs rsync 18.309 s on the same 4 GiB workload. TODO updated to track the remaining ReFS delta; plan/workflow docs reference the new numbers. **Tests/Commands**: `SOURCE_DIR=/tmp/blit_linux_bench/src DEST_DIR=/tmp/blit_linux_bench/dest SIZE_MB=1024 RUNS=5 WARMUP=1 KEEP_BENCH_DIR=1 scripts/bench_local_mirror.sh`; `SIZE_MB=1024 RUNS=5 WARMUP=1 KEEP_BENCH_DIR=1 ./scripts/bench_local_mirror_macos.sh`; `SIZE_MB=4096 RUNS=5 WARMUP=1 KEEP_BENCH_DIR=1 ./scripts/bench_local_mirror_macos.sh` (macGPT); `pwsh scripts/windows/bench-local-mirror.ps1 -SizeMB 1024 -Runs 5 -Warmup 1`; `pwsh scripts/windows/bench-local-mirror.ps1 -SizeMB 4096 -Runs 5 -Warmup 1` (WingPT); `BLIT_BIN=./target/release/blit-cli BENCH_ROOT=/mnt/specific-pool/home/stuff/backup/temp SOURCE_DIR=/mnt/specific-pool/home/stuff/backup/temp/src DEST_DIR=/mnt/specific-pool/home/stuff/backup/temp/dest SIZE_MB=4096 RUNS=5 WARMUP=1 KEEP_BENCH_DIR=1 scripts/bench_local_mirror.sh` (TrueNAS).
DEVLOG.md:371:**2025-10-26 02:06:31Z** - **RESULT**: Re-ran 4 GiB ReFS mirror with the new block-clone path on a ReFS-backed temp root (`TEMP/TMP=D:\blit_bench`). `logs/windows/bench_local_windows_4gb_clone_20251026T020337Z.log` shows blit averaging 0.590 s (best 0.385 s) vs robocopy 0.165 s (best 0.149 s)—clone is active but robocopy still leads by ~3×. Planner integration confirmed via per-file raw tasks. TODO/workflow updated; future harness work needed to avoid silently running on NTFS when env isn’t set. **Commands**: `pwsh scripts/windows/bench-local-mirror.ps1 -SizeMB 4096 -Runs 5 -Warmup 1` with `TEMP/TMP` pointed at ReFS.
DEVLOG.md:373:**2025-10-26 18:45:00Z** - **ACTION**: Windows bench harness now logs tool stderr, blit-cli emits a console line on successful `FSCTL_DUPLICATE_EXTENTS_TO_FILE`, and a prototype `BLIT_SKIP_METADATA_ON_CLONE` flag makes metadata preservation optional. Without `SeManageVolumePrivilege` the clone ioctl still falls back (bench averages ~0.68 s vs robocopy 0.19 s), but once elevated we can compare the clone-only numbers. Logged runs: `logs/windows/bench_local_windows_4gb_clone_20251026T183749Z.log` (baseline) and `bench_local_windows_4gb_clone_nometa_20251026T184100Z.log`.
DEVLOG.md:374:**2025-10-27 04:15:00Z** - **ACTION**: Completed remote tar-shard batching across TCP and gRPC paths. Extended `blit.proto` with `TarShard{Header,Chunk,Complete}` messages, taught the CLI to reuse the transfer planner for shard payloads, and upgraded both the TCP data plane and the daemon fallback path to stream shards instead of per-file payloads. Added a timeout-aware `remote_tcp_fallback` integration test (with a guard to reap the daemon) and a `tar_shard_preserves_relative_paths` unit test. Updated TODO / workflow docs to mark the milestone and captured follow-up stress-test work. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test -p blit-core`; `cargo test -p blit-cli`; `cargo check`.
DEVLOG.md:377:**2025-10-28 01:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Remote push streaming now flushes need-list batches immediately. `RemotePushClient` biases control-plane responses, streams gRPC fallback data as soon as headers land, and only emits `UploadComplete` once queues drain so the first bytes move in under one second even when the daemon negotiates fallback. Added control-plane helper for incremental sends and ensured data-plane sessions finish after the loop. **Tests**: `cargo fmt -p blit-core`; `cargo check -p blit-core`; `timeout 120s cargo test -p blit-cli remote_tcp_fallback -- --nocapture`.
DEVLOG.md:379:**2025-10-28 01:55:00Z** - **ACTION**: Instrumented remote push to capture first-payload latency. `RemotePushReport` now records the elapsed time to the first transferred payload (data-plane or fallback), and `blit-cli` prints the metric when `--progress` or `--verbose` is supplied. This supports the Phase 2 requirement to document <1 s first-byte delivery across platforms. **Tests**: `cargo fmt -p blit-core -p blit-cli`; `cargo check -p blit-core`; `cargo check -p blit-cli`.
DEVLOG.md:381:**2025-10-28 02:20:00Z** - **ACTION**: Added live throughput output for remote transfers. When CLI runs with `-p/--progress` (or `-v`), the new progress channel streams manifest batches and payload byte counts, letting the CLI print rolling MiB totals plus average/current MiB/s while data moves. Works for both TCP data-plane and gRPC fallback paths. **Tests**: `cargo fmt -p blit-core -p blit-cli`; `cargo check -p blit-core`; `cargo check -p blit-cli`; `timeout 120s cargo test -p blit-cli remote_tcp_fallback -- --nocapture`.
DEVLOG.md:389:**2025-11-06 22:55:00Z** - **ACTION**: Implemented remote→local mirror support. `blit mirror skippy://module dest/` now runs through the pull path, tracks downloaded files, and purges extraneous local entries (using `walkdir` + async removals). CLI rejects remote mirrors no longer; workflows updated. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test -p blit-cli`; `cargo test -p blit-cli --test remote_tcp_fallback`.
DEVLOG.md:392:**2025-11-10 06:20:00Z** - **PROGRESS**: Wired the remote pull path into the shared hybrid-transport stack. `proto/blit.proto` now includes pull negotiation/summary messages, the daemon’s pull service enumerates manifests, plans payloads via `remote::transfer`, and negotiates a TCP data plane (with `--force-grpc` fallback) before sending tar shards/files. `RemotePullClient` connects to the negotiated port, streams file records and tar shards to the destination, tracks downloaded paths, and captures the server summary just like push. **Tests**: `cargo build -p blit-core`; `cargo build -p blit-daemon`.
DEVLOG.md:393:**2025-11-10 07:10:00Z** - **PROGRESS**: Finished wiring the remote pull stack into the shared transport/UX. `RemotePullClient` now feeds `RemoteTransferProgress`, downloads file + tar-shard payloads over the negotiated TCP data plane (with forced gRPC fallback still supported), and surfaces the server’s summary in `describe_pull_result`. The CLI uses a single progress monitor for push/pull so `-p/-v` show manifest + throughput in both directions. TODO/plan docs updated; auto-tune scheduling for remote transfers remains the last parity gap. **Tests**: `cargo build -p blit-core`; `cargo build -p blit-daemon`.

/// Responder; an Initiator always knows its own root.
pub enum DestinationTarget {
    Fixed(PathBuf),
    Resolve(Box<OpenResolver>),
}

/// Where a SOURCE responder reads from. Symmetric with
/// [`DestinationTarget`]: `Fixed` is a source known up front (an
/// initiator's own tree, or a test), `Resolve` defers to the same
/// [`OpenResolver`] the destination path uses to map a received
/// `SessionOpen`'s module name to a local root, from which a
/// [`FsTransferSource`] is built inside blit-core (so callers stay free
/// of the concrete source type, exactly as `run_destination` builds its
/// sink from `dst_root`). A `Resolve` target is meaningful only on a
/// Responder; an Initiator always knows its own source. Used by
/// [`run_responder`] for the daemon-as-SOURCE (pull-equivalent, otp-5).
pub enum SourceResponderTarget {
    Fixed(Arc<dyn TransferSource>),
    Resolve(Box<OpenResolver>),
}

/// What a served session produced, tagged by which role the responder
/// played. `run_responder` dispatches on the initiator's declared role,
/// so the caller (the daemon) learns after the fact which half ran.
pub enum ResponderOutcome {
    /// The initiator was SOURCE; this end received (push-equivalent).
    Destination(DestinationOutcome),
    /// The initiator was DESTINATION; this end sent (pull-equivalent).
    Source(TransferSummary),
}

fn source_open_validator(open: &SessionOpen) -> std::result::Result<(), SessionFault> {
    if open.resume.as_ref().is_some_and(|r| r.enabled) {
        return Err(SessionFault::internal(
            "resume is not implemented on the unified session yet (otp-7)",
        ));
    }
    if open
        .filter
        .as_ref()
        .is_some_and(|f| *f != FilterSpec::default())
    {
        return Err(SessionFault::internal(
            "filters are not implemented on the unified session yet (otp-6)",
        ));
    }
    Ok(())
}

fn destination_open_validator(open: &SessionOpen) -> std::result::Result<(), SessionFault> {
    if open.mirror_enabled {
        return Err(SessionFault::internal(
            "mirror is not implemented on the unified session yet (otp-6)",
        ));
    }
    if open.resume.as_ref().is_some_and(|r| r.enabled) {
        return Err(SessionFault::internal(
            "resume is not implemented on the unified session yet (otp-7)",
        ));
    }
    Ok(())
}

/// Outcome of the HELLO + OPEN phases.
struct Negotiated {
    open: SessionOpen,
    /// The responder's reply. The SOURCE initiator reads
    /// `accept.data_plane` to decide dial-vs-in-stream (otp-4b).
    accept: SessionAccept,
    /// The write root a Responder's [`OpenResolver`] produced from the
    /// received open, if one was supplied; `None` for an Initiator or a
    /// fixed-root Responder (the caller supplies the root then).
    resolved_root: Option<PathBuf>,
    /// The bound data-plane listener + credentials a DESTINATION
    /// Responder prepared before its `SessionAccept` (otp-4b). `None`
    /// on an Initiator, or when the responder granted no data plane
    /// (in-stream carrier). Consumed by the DESTINATION accept loop.
    responder_data_plane: Option<data_plane::ResponderDataPlane>,
}

/// HELLO both ways, exact match (D-2026-07-05-2). First frame each
/// direction; no ordering between the two directions. Factored out so a
/// serving end (`run_responder`) can exchange HELLO, then read the OPEN
/// and dispatch on the declared role before running a role driver.
async fn exchange_hello(transport: &mut FrameTransport, hello: &HelloConfig) -> Result<()> {
    transport
        .send(frame(Frame::Hello(SessionHello {
            build_id: hello.build_id.clone(),
            contract_version: hello.contract_version,
        })))
        .await?;

    let peer_hello = match expect_frame(transport).await? {
        Frame::Hello(h) => h,
        other => {
            return Err(notify_and_wrap(
                transport,
                SessionFault::protocol_violation(format!(
                    "expected SessionHello, got {}",
                    frame_name(&Some(other))
                )),
            )
            .await)
        }
    };

    if peer_hello.build_id != hello.build_id
        || peer_hello.contract_version != hello.contract_version
    {
        let fault = SessionFault {
            code: session_error::Code::BuildMismatch,
            message: format!(
                "same-build peers required (D-2026-07-05-2): local {} (contract v{}) vs peer {} (contract v{})",
                hello.build_id, hello.contract_version,
                peer_hello.build_id, peer_hello.contract_version,
            ),
            local_build_id: hello.build_id.clone(),
            peer_build_id: peer_hello.build_id.clone(),
            peer_notified: false,
        };
        return Err(notify_and_wrap(transport, fault).await);
    }
    Ok(())
}

    // The resolver's root (Responder + Resolve) wins; otherwise the
    // caller-supplied Fixed root.
    let dst_root = match negotiated.resolved_root.clone() {
        Some(root) => root,
        None => match &target {
            DestinationTarget::Fixed(root) => root.clone(),
            // Unreachable: a Resolve target always yields a root on the
            // Responder branch, and establish only skips resolution on
            // the Initiator branch (which pairs with a Fixed root).
            DestinationTarget::Resolve(_) => {
                return Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::internal(
                    "resolver target produced no destination root",
                )));
            }
        },
    };

    drive_destination(
        &mut transport,
        negotiated,
        &dst_root,
        cfg.data_plane_host.as_deref(),
    )
    .await
}

/// The DESTINATION session body: run the diff/receive loop and map a
/// fault to a peer-notified report. Shared by [`run_destination`] and
/// [`run_responder`] (the daemon DESTINATION responder), so the receive
/// choreography is single-sourced.
async fn drive_destination(
    transport: &mut FrameTransport,
    negotiated: Negotiated,
    dst_root: &Path,
    data_plane_host: Option<&str>,
) -> Result<DestinationOutcome> {
    match destination_session(transport, negotiated, dst_root, data_plane_host).await {
        Ok(outcome) => Ok(outcome),
        Err(report) => {
            let mut fault = fault_from_report(report);
            if !fault.peer_notified {
                let _ = transport.send(error_frame(&fault)).await;
                fault.peer_notified = true;
            }
            Err(eyre::Report::new(fault))
        }
    }
}

/// Serve one transfer session as the RESPONDER, dispatching on the
/// initiator's declared role — the daemon's single serving entry
/// (contract §Invariants 3: one handshake, roles not directions). A
/// client that declares SOURCE makes this end the DESTINATION
/// (push-equivalent, otp-4); a client that declares DESTINATION makes
/// this end the SOURCE (pull-equivalent, otp-5). The two targets carry
/// the endpoint resolution for each role; only the one the initiator
/// selects is used. Returns a [`ResponderOutcome`] tagged with the role
/// that ran.
pub async fn run_responder(
    hello: HelloConfig,
    transport: FrameTransport,
    source_target: SourceResponderTarget,
    dest_target: DestinationTarget,
) -> Result<ResponderOutcome> {
    let mut transport = transport;
    exchange_hello(&mut transport, &hello).await?;
    let open = match expect_frame(&mut transport).await? {
        Frame::Open(o) => o,
        other => {
            return Err(notify_and_wrap(
                &mut transport,
                SessionFault::protocol_violation(format!(
                    "expected SessionOpen, got {}",
                    frame_name(&Some(other))
                )),
            )
            .await)
        }
    };
    let declared = TransferRole::try_from(open.initiator_role).unwrap_or(TransferRole::Unspecified);
    match declared {
        // Initiator SOURCE ⇒ this end is DESTINATION (push-equivalent).
        TransferRole::Source => {
            let resolve = match &dest_target {
                DestinationTarget::Resolve(resolver) => Some(resolver.as_ref()),
                DestinationTarget::Fixed(_) => None,
            };
            let negotiated = responder_finish(
                &mut transport,
                open,
                TransferRole::Destination,
                &destination_open_validator,
                resolve,
            )
            .await?;
            let dst_root = match negotiated.resolved_root.clone() {
                Some(root) => root,
                None => match &dest_target {
                    DestinationTarget::Fixed(root) => root.clone(),
                    DestinationTarget::Resolve(_) => {
                        return Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::internal(
                            "resolver target produced no destination root",
                        )));
                    }
                },
            };
            // A DESTINATION responder (push) binds+accepts its receive
            // sockets — it never dials, so it needs no data-plane host.
            let outcome = drive_destination(&mut transport, negotiated, &dst_root, None).await?;
            Ok(ResponderOutcome::Destination(outcome))
        }
        // Initiator DESTINATION ⇒ this end is SOURCE (pull-equivalent).
        TransferRole::Destination => {
            let resolve = match &source_target {
                SourceResponderTarget::Resolve(resolver) => Some(resolver.as_ref()),
                SourceResponderTarget::Fixed(_) => None,
            };
            let negotiated = responder_finish(
                &mut transport,
                open,
                TransferRole::Source,
                &source_open_validator,
                resolve,
            )
            .await?;
            let source: Arc<dyn TransferSource> = match source_target {
                SourceResponderTarget::Fixed(source) => source,
                SourceResponderTarget::Resolve(_) => {
                    // A Resolve target always yields a root on the
                    // Responder branch (establish only skips resolution
                    // on the Initiator branch, which uses Fixed).
                    let root = negotiated.resolved_root.clone().ok_or_else(|| {
                        eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::internal(
                            "resolver target produced no source root",
                        ))
                    })?;
                    Arc::new(FsTransferSource::new(root))
                }
            };
            // The SOURCE owns its planner knobs; a daemon-served source
            // has no client-supplied ones (§Transport selection). A SOURCE
            // responder binds+accepts its send sockets (otp-5b) — it never
            // dials, so it needs no data-plane host.
            let summary =
                drive_source(PlanOptions::default(), None, negotiated, transport, source).await?;
            Ok(ResponderOutcome::Source(summary))
        }
        TransferRole::Unspecified => Err(notify_and_wrap(
